model.dee_data_ops.mart_volume_history.name: mart_volume_history
model.dee_data_ops.mart_volume_history.description: Incremental daily snapshot of row counts for each of the three client-facing marts (sales_activity_detail, lead_journey, revenue_detail). One row per (mart, snapshot_date). Feeds the volume_drift warn-test (singular test at `2-dbt/tests/volume_drift.sql`) which compares each day's count to the prior day and surfaces mart_name / snapshot_date / pct_drift when movement exceeds ±10%. The nightly workflow runs that test with `--warn-error` in a separate step (continue-on-error) so a warn becomes a soft Slack alert without failing the build.

snapshot_date.name: snapshot_date
snapshot_date.description: UTC date the snapshot was taken (current_date() at run time).

mart_name.name: mart_name
mart_name.description: Name of the mart whose row count was captured.

row_count.name: row_count
row_count.description: Total rows in the named mart at snapshot time.

model.dee_data_ops.mart_volume_history.compiled_code:
-- Daily row-count snapshot per mart. One row per (mart, day). Feeds the
-- volume_drift warn-test which compares today's count against prior-day.
-- Marts refd here ship from Tracks F (sales_activity_detail), L (lead_journey),
-- M (revenue_detail); until all three are on main, this model will fail to
-- compile and should be excluded from CI with --exclude mart_volume_history.

with
snapshot as (
    select current_date() as snapshot_date, 'sales_activity_detail' as mart_name, count(*) as row_count
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Marts`.`sales_activity_detail`
    union all
    select current_date(), 'lead_journey', count(*)
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Marts`.`lead_journey`
    union all
    select current_date(), 'revenue_detail', count(*)
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Marts`.`revenue_detail`
)

select *
from snapshot

model.dee_data_ops.fct_outreach.name: fct_outreach
model.dee_data_ops.fct_outreach.description: Grain: one row per outbound human CALL/SMS. Source: `stg_ghl__messages` filtered to outbound CALL/SMS and joined to `stg_ghl__conversations` to require a non-null `last_manual_message_at` (excludes automation). No role filter — per Track D the SDR filter moves to the mart so this fact is reusable across SDR / AE / Closer cuts.

user_sk.name: user_sk
user_sk.description: FK to `dim_users.user_sk`. The rep who made the touch. Nullable: GHL /messages carries `user_id`s for system / automation actors that never appear in /users/search, so ~3.6k touches resolve to NULL `user_sk`. Mart-layer filters (SDR / AE leaderboards) naturally drop these via the role join; unattributed-touch diagnostics can count them.

message_id.name: message_id
message_id.description: GHL message id. Natural key for traceability.

touched_at.name: touched_at
touched_at.description: UTC timestamp the outbound touch was sent (`sent_at`).

channel.name: channel
channel.description: `call` when source `message_type = 'TYPE_CALL'`; `sms` when `TYPE_SMS`. No other values possible post-filter.

match_method.name: match_method
match_method.description: Always `ghl_native` — messages carry `contact_id`/`user_id` directly from GHL, so no matching heuristic is needed.

model.dee_data_ops.fct_outreach.raw_code:
-- Grain: one row per outbound human CALL/SMS. Source is
-- `stg_ghl__messages` filtered to `direction = 'outbound'` and message_type
-- in ('TYPE_CALL','TYPE_SMS'). Automation is excluded by joining to
-- `stg_ghl__conversations` and requiring `last_manual_message_at is not
-- null` at the conversation level — a manual-touch signal GHL surfaces
-- natively. No role filter here: per Track D's decisions the SDR filter
-- moves to the mart so this fact stays reusable for AE/Closer analysis.
--
-- contact match is GHL-native (`contact_id` on the message row) so
-- match_method = 'ghl_native', match_score = 1.00 for every row.
-- user_sk is resolved via LEFT JOIN to dim_users rather than hashed
-- directly from the message's user_id, because GHL /messages carries
-- user_ids for system/automation actors that never appear in
-- /users/search. Those rows get user_sk NULL (unattributed) instead
-- of emitting a dangling SK that breaks the relationships test.

with

messages as (

    select *
    from {{ ref('stg_ghl__messages') }}
    where lower(direction) = 'outbound'
        and message_type in ('TYPE_CALL', 'TYPE_SMS')

),

conversations as (

    select
        conversation_id,
        last_manual_message_at
    from {{ ref('stg_ghl__conversations') }}

),

manual_touches as (

    select
        messages.*
    from messages
    inner join conversations
        on messages.conversation_id = conversations.conversation_id
    where conversations.last_manual_message_at is not null

),

users as (

    select
        user_id,
        user_sk
    from {{ ref('dim_users') }}

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['manual_touches.message_id']) }}
                                                                as touch_sk,

        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(
            ['manual_touches.location_id', 'manual_touches.contact_id']
        ) }}                                                    as contact_sk,

        users.user_sk,

        manual_touches.message_id,
        manual_touches.conversation_id,
        manual_touches.location_id,

        manual_touches.sent_at                                  as touched_at,

        case
            when manual_touches.message_type = 'TYPE_CALL' then 'call'
            when manual_touches.message_type = 'TYPE_SMS'  then 'sms'
        end                                                     as channel,

        manual_touches.message_source,
        manual_touches.status                                   as message_status,

        'ghl_native'                                            as match_method,
        1.00                                                    as match_score

    from manual_touches
    left join users
        on manual_touches.user_id = users.user_id

)

select * from final
model.dee_data_ops.fct_outreach.compiled_code:
-- Grain: one row per outbound human CALL/SMS. Source is
-- `stg_ghl__messages` filtered to `direction = 'outbound'` and message_type
-- in ('TYPE_CALL','TYPE_SMS'). Automation is excluded by joining to
-- `stg_ghl__conversations` and requiring `last_manual_message_at is not
-- null` at the conversation level — a manual-touch signal GHL surfaces
-- natively. No role filter here: per Track D's decisions the SDR filter
-- moves to the mart so this fact stays reusable for AE/Closer analysis.
--
-- contact match is GHL-native (`contact_id` on the message row) so
-- match_method = 'ghl_native', match_score = 1.00 for every row.
-- user_sk is resolved via LEFT JOIN to dim_users rather than hashed
-- directly from the message's user_id, because GHL /messages carries
-- user_ids for system/automation actors that never appear in
-- /users/search. Those rows get user_sk NULL (unattributed) instead
-- of emitting a dangling SK that breaks the relationships test.

with

messages as (

    select *
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__messages`
    where lower(direction) = 'outbound'
        and message_type in ('TYPE_CALL', 'TYPE_SMS')

),

conversations as (

    select
        conversation_id,
        last_manual_message_at
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__conversations`

),

manual_touches as (

    select
        messages.*
    from messages
    inner join conversations
        on messages.conversation_id = conversations.conversation_id
    where conversations.last_manual_message_at is not null

),

users as (

    select
        user_id,
        user_sk
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_users`

),

final as (

    select
        to_hex(md5(cast(coalesce(cast(manual_touches.message_id as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as string)))
                                                                as touch_sk,

        to_hex(md5(cast(coalesce(cast(manual_touches.location_id as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') || '-' || coalesce(cast(manual_touches.contact_id as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as string)))                                                    as contact_sk,

        users.user_sk,

        manual_touches.message_id,
        manual_touches.conversation_id,
        manual_touches.location_id,

        manual_touches.sent_at                                  as touched_at,

        case
            when manual_touches.message_type = 'TYPE_CALL' then 'call'
            when manual_touches.message_type = 'TYPE_SMS'  then 'sms'
        end                                                     as channel,

        manual_touches.message_source,
        manual_touches.status                                   as message_status,

        'ghl_native'                                            as match_method,
        1.00                                                    as match_score

    from manual_touches
    left join users
        on manual_touches.user_id = users.user_id

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.fct_refunds.name: fct_refunds
model.dee_data_ops.fct_refunds.description:
Grain: one row per refund event. PK: `refund_sk`. **WARNING: Fanbasis-only — excludes Stripe historical refunds.** `stg_stripe__charges.amount_refunded_minor` is at charge-grain (one number per charge, not one row per refund event), so a Stripe arm would lose temporal fidelity. Aggregating `sum(refund_amount)` from this fact answers "live forward-going refunds since Fanbasis cutover," not "total refunds in D-DEE history." See `fct_refunds__overview` doc block before using this fact in cross-period analysis.
Contact attribution via `bridge_identity_contact_payment` joined on the **parent** payment's `(source_platform, payment_id)` — refunds inherit their contact from the original payment, so we do not re-run identity matching on the refund row.

contact_sk.name: contact_sk
contact_sk.description: FK to `dim_contacts.contact_sk`. Nullable when bridge returns `unmatched`.

parent_payment_id.name: parent_payment_id
parent_payment_id.description: Native id of the payment being refunded. Joins to `fct_payments.payment_id` at `(source_platform, parent_payment_id) = (source_platform, payment_id)`.

refunded_at.name: refunded_at
refunded_at.description: UTC timestamp of the refund event.

refund_amount.name: refund_amount
refund_amount.description: Gross refund amount in USD (major units) returned to the customer.

refund_amount_net.name: refund_amount_net
refund_amount_net.description: Refund amount minus the processor fee, in USD. Can be negative if processor fee exceeds the gross refund (rare but legitimate on chargebacks).

refund_fee.name: refund_fee
refund_fee.description: Processor fee charged on the refund, in USD.

refund_total_cost.name: refund_total_cost
refund_total_cost.description: Total cost to merchant per Fanbasis (refund_amount + processor side-effects), in USD.

match_method.name: match_method
match_method.description: Bridge tier that produced the contact attribution (or `unmatched`).

model.dee_data_ops.fct_refunds.raw_code:
-- Grain: one row per refund event (Fanbasis-only).
-- PK: `refund_sk`, surrogate over `(source_platform, refund_id)`.
--
-- Fanbasis is the only event-grain refund source today. Stripe staging
-- exposes only `amount_refunded_minor` aggregated per charge — that's
-- charge-grain, not refund-event-grain, so a Stripe arm here would
-- require an extractor change first. Stripe is also banned at D-DEE
-- (memory `project_stripe_historical_only.md`) so the asymmetry is
-- correct in practice: live forward-going refunds are 100% Fanbasis.
-- If Stripe historical refund timing ever matters, scaffold a separate
-- `stg_stripe__refunds` from the Stripe payload first; do not collapse
-- charge-grain refund_amount into this table (would silently double-
-- count multi-refund charges and lose temporal fidelity).
--
-- Contact attribution: refunds inherit the contact from the parent
-- payment, so the bridge is joined on `(source_platform='fanbasis',
-- parent payment_id)` — the same composite key `fct_payments.payment_sk`
-- is hashed over. This is not a fact-to-fact join (the bridge is not a
-- fact); the parent payment is referenced by its native id, not via
-- `fct_payments.payment_sk`.

with

fanbasis_refunds as (

    select
        refund_id,
        payment_id                                            as parent_payment_id,
        'fanbasis'                                            as source_platform,

        refund_amount,
        refund_amount_net,
        refund_fee,
        refund_total_cost,
        currency,

        refunded_at
    from {{ ref('stg_fanbasis__refunds') }}

),

bridge as (

    select
        source_platform,
        payment_id,
        contact_sk,
        match_method,
        match_score,
        bridge_status
    from {{ ref('bridge_identity_contact_payment') }}

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(
            ['fanbasis_refunds.source_platform', 'fanbasis_refunds.refund_id']
        ) }}                                                  as refund_sk,

        bridge.contact_sk,

        fanbasis_refunds.refund_id,
        fanbasis_refunds.parent_payment_id,
        fanbasis_refunds.source_platform,

        fanbasis_refunds.refunded_at,

        fanbasis_refunds.refund_amount,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_amount_net,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_fee,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_total_cost,
        fanbasis_refunds.currency,

        coalesce(bridge.match_method,  'unmatched')           as match_method,
        coalesce(bridge.match_score,   0.00)                  as match_score,
        coalesce(bridge.bridge_status, 'unmatched')           as bridge_status

)

model.dee_data_ops.fct_refunds.compiled_code:
-- Grain: one row per refund event (Fanbasis-only).
-- PK: `refund_sk`, surrogate over `(source_platform, refund_id)`.
--
-- Fanbasis is the only event-grain refund source today. Stripe staging
-- exposes only `amount_refunded_minor` aggregated per charge — that's
-- charge-grain, not refund-event-grain, so a Stripe arm here would
-- require an extractor change first. Stripe is also banned at D-DEE
-- (memory `project_stripe_historical_only.md`) so the asymmetry is
-- correct in practice: live forward-going refunds are 100% Fanbasis.
-- If Stripe historical refund timing ever matters, scaffold a separate
-- `stg_stripe__refunds` from the Stripe payload first; do not collapse
-- charge-grain refund_amount into this table (would silently double-
-- count multi-refund charges and lose temporal fidelity).
--
-- Contact attribution: refunds inherit the contact from the parent
-- payment, so the bridge is joined on `(source_platform='fanbasis',
-- parent payment_id)` — the same composite key `fct_payments.payment_sk`
-- is hashed over. This is not a fact-to-fact join (the bridge is not a
-- fact); the parent payment is referenced by its native id, not via
-- `fct_payments.payment_sk`.

with

fanbasis_refunds as (

    select
        refund_id,
        payment_id                                            as parent_payment_id,
        'fanbasis'                                            as source_platform,

        refund_amount,
        refund_amount_net,
        refund_fee,
        refund_total_cost,
        currency,

        refunded_at
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_fanbasis__refunds`

),

bridge as (

    select
        source_platform,
        payment_id,
        contact_sk,
        match_method,
        match_score,
        bridge_status
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`bridge_identity_contact_payment`

),

final as (

    select
        to_hex(md5(cast(coalesce(cast(fanbasis_refunds.source_platform as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') || '-' || coalesce(cast(fanbasis_refunds.refund_id as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as string)))                                                  as refund_sk,

        bridge.contact_sk,

        fanbasis_refunds.refund_id,
        fanbasis_refunds.parent_payment_id,
        fanbasis_refunds.source_platform,

        fanbasis_refunds.refunded_at,

        fanbasis_refunds.refund_amount,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_amount_net,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_fee,
        fanbasis_refunds.refund_total_cost,
        fanbasis_refunds.currency,

        coalesce(bridge.match_method,  'unmatched')           as match_method,
        coalesce(bridge.match_score,   0.00)                  as match_score,
        coalesce(bridge.bridge_status, 'unmatched')           as bridge_status

    from fanbasis_refunds
    left join bridge
        on bridge.source_platform = fanbasis_refunds.source_platform
       and bridge.payment_id      = fanbasis_refunds.parent_payment_id

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.fct_calls_booked.name: fct_calls_booked
model.dee_data_ops.fct_calls_booked.description: Grain: one row per Calendly booking event — the Speed-to-Lead denominator source. Source: `stg_calendly__events`. `contact_sk` resolves through `stg_calendly__event_invitees.invitee_email_norm` → `dim_contacts.email_norm`; bookings without a matching GHL contact resolve to NULL contact_sk (relationships test auto-excludes NULLs). `assigned_user_sk` + `pipeline_stage_sk` resolve via the most-recent GHL opportunity for the contact whose `opportunity_created_at <= booked_at` ("active opp at booking time") — *diagnostic* attribution only. The headline Speed-to-Lead numerator sources first-touch identity from `raw_ghl.conversations` / `fct_outreach`, independent of opportunity state. Bookings whose contact has no pre-booking opportunity resolve to NULL on both attribution SKs.

contact_sk.name: contact_sk
contact_sk.description: FK to `dim_contacts.contact_sk`. Nullable when the booking's invitee email doesn't match a GHL contact.

assigned_user_sk.name: assigned_user_sk
assigned_user_sk.description: FK to `dim_users.user_sk`. Resolved via the most-recent GHL opportunity for the contact whose `opportunity_created_at <= booked_at`. NULL when the contact has no pre-booking opportunity. Diagnostic attribution only — not on the Speed-to-Lead numerator path.

pipeline_stage_sk.name: pipeline_stage_sk
pipeline_stage_sk.description: FK to `dim_pipeline_stages.pipeline_stage_sk`. Resolved from the same active-opp-at-booking-time as `assigned_user_sk`. Nullable on the same condition.

calendly_event_id.name: calendly_event_id
calendly_event_id.description: Raw Calendly event id (`uri`). Natural key for traceability.

booked_at.name: booked_at
booked_at.description: UTC timestamp the invitee confirmed the slot. Speed-to-Lead start clock.

scheduled_for.name: scheduled_for
scheduled_for.description: UTC timestamp the meeting is / was scheduled to start.

cancelled_at.name: cancelled_at
cancelled_at.description: UTC timestamp of cancellation when `event_status` is cancelled.

model.dee_data_ops.fct_calls_booked.raw_code:
-- Grain: one row per Calendly booking event (`stg_calendly__events.event_id`).
-- Speed-to-Lead denominator source — every confirmed booking, including
-- bookings that never landed on a GHL opportunity.
--
-- contact_sk resolution: `stg_calendly__event_invitees.invitee_email_norm`
-- → `dim_contacts.email_norm` → `contact_sk`. Bookings whose invitee email
-- doesn't match a GHL contact resolve to NULL contact_sk (the relationships
-- test auto-excludes NULLs). Do not widen `dim_contacts` here.
--
-- assigned_user_sk + pipeline_stage_sk are *diagnostic* attribution — they
-- represent which SDR GHL believes owns the booking, NOT the headline
-- Speed-to-Lead numerator (which sources first-touch identity from
-- raw_ghl.conversations / fct_outreach, independent of opportunity state).
-- The selected opportunity is the most-recent one for the contact whose
-- `opportunity_created_at <= booked_at` ("active opp at booking time"),
-- with `opportunity_id desc` as the deterministic tiebreaker on ties.
-- If no opp pre-existed the booking, both SKs are NULL. The strict `<=`
-- boundary is sub-second sensitive: if the GHL "Booked" workflow creates
-- an opp T+50ms after the Calendly booking event, the fact selects the
-- *prior* opp on this contact (or NULL) — by design, since this column
-- represents the SDR who owned the contact *before* the booking, not the
-- workflow-created opp. This rule replaces three divergent rules that were
-- coexisting in marts (sales_activity_detail used "latest opp by created_at"
-- with a broken time filter; lead_journey used "latest opp by updated_at");
-- follow-up PR will collapse the marts to consume these SKs from the fact.

with

events as (

    select * from {{ ref('stg_calendly__events') }}

),

invitees as (

    select * from {{ ref('stg_calendly__event_invitees') }}

),

contacts as (

    select
        contact_sk,
        contact_id,
        email_norm
    from {{ ref('dim_contacts') }}

),

opportunities as (

    select
        opportunity_id,
        contact_id,
        assigned_user_id,
        pipeline_id,
        pipeline_stage_id,
        opportunity_created_at
    from {{ ref('stg_ghl__opportunities') }}

),

users as (

    select
        user_id,
        user_sk
    from {{ ref('dim_users') }}

),

pipeline_stages as (

    select
        pipeline_id,
        stage_id,
        pipeline_stage_sk
    from {{ ref('dim_pipeline_stages') }}

),

-- Resolve booking → contact_id, plus the active opp at booking time.
booking_contact as (

    select
        events.event_id,
        events.booked_at,
        contacts.contact_sk,
        contacts.contact_id
    from events
    left join invitees
        on invitees.event_id = events.event_id
    left join contacts
        on contacts.email_norm = invitees.invitee_email_norm

),

opportunity_at_booking as (

    select
        booking_contact.event_id,
        opportunities.assigned_user_id,
        opportunities.pipeline_id,
        opportunities.pipeline_stage_id
    from booking_contact
    left join opportunities
        on opportunities.contact_id = booking_contact.contact_id
       and opportunities.opportunity_created_at <= booking_contact.booked_at
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by booking_contact.event_id
        order by opportunities.opportunity_created_at desc,
                 opportunities.opportunity_id desc
    ) = 1

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['events.event_id']) }}
                                                                as booking_sk,

        booking_contact.contact_sk                              as contact_sk,
        users.user_sk                                           as assigned_user_sk,
        pipeline_stages.pipeline_stage_sk                       as pipeline_stage_sk,

        events.event_id                                         as calendly_event_id,
        events.event_type_id,
        events.event_name,

        events.booked_at,
        events.scheduled_for,
        events.scheduled_until,

        events.status                                           as event_status,

        case
            when lower(events.status) in ('canceled', 'cancelled')
            then events.updated_at
            else null
        end                                                     as cancelled_at,

        events.location_type,
        events.active_invitee_count,
        events.is_deleted

    from events
    left join booking_contact
        on booking_contact.event_id = events.event_id
    left join opportunity_at_booking
        on opportunity_at_booking.event_id = events.event_id
    left join users
        on users.user_id = opportunity_at_booking.assigned_user_id
    left join pipeline_stages
        on pipeline_stages.pipeline_id = opportunity_at_booking.pipeline_id
       and pipeline_stages.stage_id    = opportunity_at_booking.pipeline_stage_id

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.fct_payments.name: fct_payments
model.dee_data_ops.fct_payments.description: Grain: one row per payment event, unioned across Stripe and Fanbasis. Stripe rows are historical-only (Stripe banned at D-DEE per memory `project_stripe_historical_only.md`); Fanbasis is the live forward-going contributor (~466 rows / $170K gross as of 2026-04-27). Contact attribution flows through `bridge_identity_contact_payment`, joined on `(source_platform, payment_id)`; `contact_sk` is nullable when the bridge returns `unmatched`.

payment_id.name: payment_id
payment_id.description: Native payment id (Stripe `charge_id` or Fanbasis transaction id).

gross_amount.name: gross_amount
gross_amount.description: Gross amount in major units (e.g. USD). Charge amount before refunds.

net_amount.name: net_amount
net_amount.description: Net amount in major units — captured minus refunded.

product.name: product
product.description: Free-text product description from the payment.

payment_method.name: payment_method
payment_method.description: Channel metadata from the source (e.g. Stripe `outcome_network_status`).

card_issue_country.name: card_issue_country
card_issue_country.description: ISO country code from billing address.

match_score.name: match_score
match_score.description: Confidence score attached to the chosen tier (0.00-1.00).

model.dee_data_ops.fct_payments.raw_code:
-- Grain: one row per payment event, unioned across Stripe + Fanbasis.
-- Stripe rows are historical-only (Stripe banned at D-DEE per memory
-- `project_stripe_historical_only.md`); Fanbasis is the live forward-going
-- contributor.
--
-- `contact_sk` + bridge metadata come from
-- `bridge_identity_contact_payment`, joined on `(source_platform,
-- payment_id)` so a Stripe `charge_id` and a Fanbasis `payment_id` cannot
-- collide. Amounts land in major units (USD here; non-USD currency rows
-- pass through at face value — multi-currency handling is a mart-layer
-- concern).

with

stripe_payments as (

    select
        charge_id                                             as payment_id,
        'stripe'                                              as source_platform,

        cast(amount_minor as numeric) / 100                   as gross_amount,
        cast(amount_captured_minor - coalesce(amount_refunded_minor, 0) as numeric)
            / 100                                             as net_amount,
        currency,

        description                                           as product,

        billing_email,
        billing_country                                       as card_issue_country,
        outcome_network_status                                as payment_method,

        charged_at                                            as transaction_date,
        is_captured,
        is_paid,
        is_refunded
    from {{ ref('stg_stripe__charges') }}

),

fanbasis_payments as (

    select
        payment_id                                            as payment_id,
        'fanbasis'                                            as source_platform,

        gross_amount,
        net_amount,
        currency,

        product_title                                         as product,

        fan_email                                             as billing_email,
        fan_country_code                                      as card_issue_country,
        payment_type                                          as payment_method,

        transaction_date,
        is_captured,
        is_paid,
        is_refunded
    from {{ ref('stg_fanbasis__transactions') }}

),

unioned as (

    select * from stripe_payments
    union all
    select * from fanbasis_payments

),

bridge as (

    select
        source_platform,
        payment_id,
        contact_sk,
        match_method,
        match_score,
        bridge_status
    from {{ ref('bridge_identity_contact_payment') }}

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(
            ['unioned.source_platform', 'unioned.payment_id']
        ) }}                                                  as payment_sk,

        bridge.contact_sk,

        unioned.payment_id,
        unioned.source_platform,

        unioned.transaction_date,
        unioned.gross_amount,
        unioned.net_amount,
        unioned.currency,

        unioned.product,
        unioned.payment_method,
        unioned.card_issue_country,

        unioned.is_captured,
        unioned.is_paid,
        unioned.is_refunded,

        coalesce(bridge.match_method,  'unmatched')           as match_method,
        coalesce(bridge.match_score,   0.00)                  as match_score,
        coalesce(bridge.bridge_status, 'unmatched')           as bridge_status

    from unioned
    left join bridge
        on unioned.source_platform = bridge.source_platform
       and unioned.payment_id      = bridge.payment_id

)

select * from final

        bridge.contact_sk,

    left join bridge
        on unioned.source_platform = bridge.source_platform
       and unioned.payment_id      = bridge.payment_id

model.dee_data_ops.fct_speed_to_lead_tou.name: fct_speed_to_lead_touch
model.dee_data_ops.fct_speed_to_lead_tou.description:
One row per (booking × touch-event). Lowest-grain fact for Speed-to-Lead.
Bookings with zero SDR touches emit one row with touch_sk = NULL.
Resolves the show_outcome-fallback DQ gap noted in .claude/state/project-state.md.
(source: "3 Data Modeling Mistakes That Can Derail a Team", Data Ops notebook —
grain justification for going to the lowest level.)

touch_sk.name: touch_sk
touch_sk.description: FK to fct_outreach.touch_sk. NULL when booking has no matched outbound SDR touch — semantically meaningful.

user_sk.name: user_sk
user_sk.description: FK to dim_users.user_sk. The rep who made the touch. NULL when touch_sk IS NULL or the user is not in dim_users (roster gaps).

sdr_sk.name: sdr_sk
sdr_sk.description: FK to dim_sdr.sdr_sk. Populated only when user_sk resolves to a user with role = 'SDR'. NULL for non-SDR touches.

source_sk.name: source_sk
source_sk.description: FK to dim_source.source_sk. Never NULL — __unknown__ sentinel row handles missing lead_source.

booked_at.name: booked_at
booked_at.description: UTC timestamp of the Calendly booking event. Speed-to-Lead start clock.

touched_at.name: touched_at
touched_at.description: UTC timestamp of the outbound touch. NULL when touch_sk is NULL.

minutes_to_touch.name: minutes_to_touch
minutes_to_touch.description: Elapsed minutes from booked_at to touched_at. NULL when no touch.

is_first_touch.name: is_first_touch
is_first_touch.description: TRUE on exactly one row per booking — the OVERALL earliest outbound touch row, regardless of toucher role. FALSE for subsequent touches and no-touch rows. Paired with `is_sdr_touch`, `is_first_touch AND is_sdr_touch` reproduces the legacy `sales_activity_detail.first_toucher_role = 'SDR'` filter for the locked 2026-04-19 headline metric.

is_sdr_touch.name: is_sdr_touch
is_sdr_touch.description: TRUE when the touching user's current role is 'SDR'. Uses current-state dim_users join (v1); SCD-2 as-of join deferred to F2.

channel.name: channel
channel.description: Touch channel — 'call', 'sms', or NULL when touch_sk is NULL.

is_within_5_min_sla.name: is_within_5_min_sla
is_within_5_min_sla.description: TRUE when is_first_touch AND is_sdr_touch AND minutes_to_touch < 5. The headline Speed-to-Lead metric numerator.

had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr.name: had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr
had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr.description: Booking-grain flag — TRUE if any SDR touch landed within 60 min of booked_at, regardless of which touch row this is.

event_status.name: event_status
event_status.description: Calendly event status (active, canceled, etc.) from fct_calls_booked.

cancelled_at.name: cancelled_at
cancelled_at.description: Cancellation timestamp when event_status indicates cancellation.

show_outcome.name: show_outcome
show_outcome.description: showed / no_show / cancelled / pending. Derived from Calendly event_status + GHL opportunity last_stage_change_at. v1 heuristic; fallback documented in model comment for F3 finalization.

close_outcome.name: close_outcome
close_outcome.description: won / lost / pending. Mirrors the closer_and_outcome CTE in sales_activity_detail.

closed_at.name: closed_at
closed_at.description: UTC timestamp of won/lost status change on the linked opportunity.

lost_reason.name: lost_reason
lost_reason.description: Raw GHL lost_reason_id. FK to lost-reason lookup (v1 raw id; enrichment deferred).

attribution_quality_flag.name: attribution_quality_flag
attribution_quality_flag.description: no_sdr_touch / role_unknown / pre_utm_era / clean. Verbatim logic from sales_activity_detail attribution_quality_flag column.

attribution_era.name: attribution_era
attribution_era.description: pre_UTM or utm — passed through from dim_contacts.

era_flag.name: era_flag
era_flag.description: ramping (booked_at < 2026-03-16) or stable (>= 2026-03-16). Matches sales_activity_detail era_flag logic.

mart_refreshed_at.name: mart_refreshed_at
mart_refreshed_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent dbt build for this row.

model.dee_data_ops.fct_speed_to_lead_tou.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        partition_by={
            'field': 'booked_at',
            'data_type': 'timestamp',
            'granularity': 'day'
        }
    )
}}

-- Grain: one row per (booking_sk, touch_sk). Bookings with zero outbound
-- human SDR touches emit ONE row with touch_sk = NULL — this preserves
-- denominator counts for booking-level rollups (% of bookings with ≥1 touch).
--
-- Justification for going to this grain: "If you have the option to go to a
-- lower granularity and you can still achieve the same result I tend to
-- recommend doing that — it gives you more options... because you can then
-- roll things up." (source: "3 Data Modeling Mistakes That Can Derail a Team",
-- Data Ops notebook.) The mart-layer fallback `close_outcome IS NOT NULL` in
-- `sales_activity_detail` is exactly the "missing out on the ability to report
-- on data because you didn't go low enough" failure-mode that source calls out.
-- Resolution: this fact carries a real `show_outcome` column per touch row,
-- resolving the DQ gap noted in .claude/state/project-state.md.
--
-- show_outcome derivation (v1): Calendly event_status + GHL opportunity
-- last_stage_change_at signal. Heuristic: if the opportunity stage changed
-- AFTER scheduled_for, we treat that as "attended" (the rep progressed the
-- stage because the call happened). This is a v1 approximation; the fallback
-- for rows where no opportunity exists or stage_change is absent is documented
-- below with a code comment and flagged in WORKLOG "Open threads" for F3
-- finalization.
--
-- SCD-2 note: is_sdr_touch uses dim_users.role at query time (current-state
-- join). For historical as-of joins use dim_users_snapshot with the
-- dbt_valid_from / dbt_valid_to window. F2 will layer in the SCD join for the
-- mart; F1 uses current-state role to keep the fact buildable before the
-- snapshot has history.

with

bookings as (

    select
        booking_sk,
        contact_sk,
        booked_at,
        scheduled_for,
        event_status,
        cancelled_at
    from {{ ref('fct_calls_booked') }}

),

touches as (

    select
        touch_sk,
        contact_sk,
        user_sk,
        touched_at,
        channel
    from {{ ref('fct_outreach') }}

),

users as (

    select
        user_sk,
        user_id,
        name,
        role,
        is_active
    from {{ ref('dim_users') }}

),

contacts as (

    select
        contact_sk,
        contact_id,
        lead_source,
        attribution_era
    from {{ ref('dim_contacts') }}

),

-- Pick the opportunity closest to (and after) booked_at for the contact.
-- If none after, fall back to most recent before (legacy import coverage).
-- Mirrors the closer_and_outcome CTE in sales_activity_detail.
opportunities as (

    select
        contact_id,
        status,
        last_status_change_at,
        last_stage_change_at,
        lost_reason_id,
        opportunity_created_at
    from {{ ref('stg_ghl__opportunities') }}

),

calendar as (

    select
        date_sk,
        date_day
    from {{ ref('dim_calendar_dates') }}

),

dim_sdr as (

    select
        sdr_sk,
        user_sk
    from {{ ref('dim_sdr') }}

),

dim_src as (

    select
        source_sk,
        lead_source
    from {{ ref('dim_source') }}

),

-- Left-join touches to bookings on contact match + touched AFTER booked
-- This is the same axis as the first_touch CTE in sales_activity_detail.
booking_touches as (

    select
        bookings.booking_sk,
        bookings.contact_sk,
        bookings.booked_at,
        bookings.scheduled_for,
        bookings.event_status,
        bookings.cancelled_at,
        touches.touch_sk,
        touches.user_sk,
        touches.touched_at,
        touches.channel
    from bookings
    left join touches
        on touches.contact_sk = bookings.contact_sk
       and touches.touched_at >= bookings.booked_at

),

-- Resolve opportunity per booking (dedup to one row per booking_sk)
booking_opp as (

    select
        bookings.booking_sk,
        opps.status                         as opp_status,
        opps.last_status_change_at,
        opps.last_stage_change_at,
        opps.lost_reason_id,
        opps.opportunity_created_at
    from bookings
    left join contacts c
        on c.contact_sk = bookings.contact_sk
    left join opportunities opps
        on opps.contact_id = c.contact_id
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by bookings.booking_sk
        order by opps.opportunity_created_at desc
    ) = 1

),

-- Compute had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr at booking grain (repeated per row)
had_activity_1hr as (

    select
        bt.booking_sk,
        count(bt.touch_sk) > 0              as had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr
    from booking_touches bt
    inner join users u
        on u.user_sk = bt.user_sk
       and lower(u.role) = 'sdr'
    where bt.touched_at between bt.booked_at
                             and timestamp_add(bt.booked_at, interval 60 minute)
    group by bt.booking_sk

),

enriched as (

    select
        bt.booking_sk,
        bt.contact_sk,
        bt.booked_at,
        bt.scheduled_for,
        bt.event_status,
        bt.cancelled_at,
        bt.touch_sk,
        bt.user_sk,
        bt.touched_at,
        bt.channel,

        u.role                                                  as toucher_role,

        -- is_sdr_touch: current-state role join (v1; SCD-2 as-of join in F2)
        lower(u.role) = 'sdr'                                   as is_sdr_touch,

        -- is_first_touch: TRUE on the OVERALL earliest touch per booking,
        -- regardless of toucher role. Aligned to the 2026-04-19 locked metric
        -- definition — "SDR-attributed denominator" = the SDR was the first
        -- toucher (if a non-SDR touched first, the booking attributes to that
        -- role, not the SDR). Paired with `is_sdr_touch`, `is_first_touch AND
        -- is_sdr_touch` reproduces the old `first_toucher_role = 'SDR'` filter
        -- on sales_activity_detail. See stl_headline_parity test for the
        -- regression gate.
        case
            when bt.touched_at is not null
                then row_number() over (
                    partition by bt.booking_sk
                    order by bt.touched_at asc
                ) = 1
            else false
        end                                                     as is_first_touch,

        bo.opp_status,
        bo.last_status_change_at,
        bo.last_stage_change_at,
        bo.lost_reason_id,

        coalesce(ha.had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr, false)    as had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,

        c.lead_source,
        c.attribution_era,

        ds.sdr_sk,
        src.source_sk

    from booking_touches bt
    left join users u           on u.user_sk = bt.user_sk
    left join booking_opp bo    on bo.booking_sk = bt.booking_sk
    left join had_activity_1hr ha on ha.booking_sk = bt.booking_sk
    left join contacts c        on c.contact_sk = bt.contact_sk
    left join dim_sdr ds        on ds.user_sk = bt.user_sk
    left join dim_src src       on src.lead_source = c.lead_source

),

final as (

    select
        -- PK: coalesce touch_sk so null-touch rows still get a unique SK
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key([
            'booking_sk', "coalesce(touch_sk, 'no-touch')"
        ]) }}                                                   as speed_to_lead_touch_sk,

        booking_sk,
        touch_sk,
        contact_sk,
        user_sk,
        sdr_sk,

        coalesce(
            source_sk,
            -- __unknown__ row in dim_source handles NULLs
            (
                select source_sk from dim_src
                where lead_source = '__unknown__'
                limit 1
            )
        )                                                       as source_sk,

        -- date_sk: join on date of booking
        cal.date_sk,

        booked_at,
        touched_at,

        case
            when touched_at is not null
                then timestamp_diff(touched_at, booked_at, minute)
        end                                                     as minutes_to_touch,

        is_first_touch,
        is_sdr_touch,
        channel,

        -- is_within_5_min_sla: first SDR touch that arrived within 5 min
        case
            when is_first_touch and is_sdr_touch and touched_at is not null
                then timestamp_diff(touched_at, booked_at, minute) < 5
            else false
        end                                                     as is_within_5_min_sla,

        had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,
        event_status,
        cancelled_at,

        -- show_outcome: v1 heuristic from Calendly event_status + GHL stage signal
        -- Fallback (v1 approximation): when no opportunity exists for a booking,
        -- or when last_stage_change_at is absent, we cannot apply the stage-change
        -- heuristic. We fall back to `close_outcome IS NOT NULL` as a weak
        -- "showed" signal. This is documented here so F3 can find and finalize it.
        -- OPEN THREAD: validate heuristic accuracy against ~20 manual inspections
        -- before F2 ships (flagged in WORKLOG "Open threads").
        case
            when lower(event_status) in ('canceled', 'cancelled')
                then 'cancelled'
            when event_status = 'active'
                 and current_timestamp() < scheduled_for
                then 'pending'
            when event_status = 'active'
                 and current_timestamp() >= scheduled_for
                 and last_stage_change_at is not null
                 and last_stage_change_at >= scheduled_for
                then 'showed'
            when event_status = 'active'
                 and current_timestamp() >= scheduled_for
                 and last_stage_change_at is null
                -- v1 fallback: no stage signal available; treat close outcome as
                -- showed proxy. See code comment above re: F3 finalization.
                and lower(opp_status) in ('won', 'lost')
                then 'showed'
            else 'no_show'
        end                                                     as show_outcome,

        -- close_outcome: mirrors closer_and_outcome CTE in sales_activity_detail
        case
            when lower(opp_status) = 'won'  then 'won'
            when lower(opp_status) = 'lost' then 'lost'
            else 'pending'
        end                                                     as close_outcome,

        case
            when lower(opp_status) in ('won', 'lost')
                then last_status_change_at
        end                                                     as closed_at,

        lost_reason_id                                          as lost_reason,

        -- attribution_quality_flag: verbatim from sales_activity_detail lines 225-234
        case
            when touched_at is null and toucher_role is null
                then 'no_sdr_touch'
            when toucher_role is null
              or lower(toucher_role) = 'unknown'
                then 'role_unknown'
            when attribution_era = 'pre_UTM'
                then 'pre_utm_era'
            else 'clean'
        end                                                     as attribution_quality_flag,

        attribution_era,

        -- era_flag: inline CASE matching sales_activity_detail lines 236-239
        case
            when date(booked_at) < date '2026-03-16' then 'ramping'
            else 'stable'
        end                                                     as era_flag,

        current_timestamp()                                     as mart_refreshed_at

    from enriched
    left join calendar cal
        on cal.date_day = date(booked_at)

)

select * from final

              or lower(toucher_role) = 'unknown'
                then 'role_unknown'
            when attribution_era = 'pre_UTM'
                then 'pre_utm_era'
            else 'clean'
        end                                                     as attribution_quality_flag,

        attribution_era,

        -- era_flag: inline CASE matching sales_activity_detail lines 236-239
        case
            when date(booked_at) < date '2026-03-16' then 'ramping'
            else 'stable'
        end                                                     as era_flag,

        current_timestamp()                                     as mart_refreshed_at

    from enriched
    left join calendar cal
        on cal.date_day = date(booked_at)

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.dim_sdr.name: dim_sdr
model.dee_data_ops.dim_sdr.description: SDR-role-filtered conformed dimension over dim_users. One row per currently-active SDR. Not a duplicate dimension — a role-scoped subset view, per the conformed-dimension pattern. Carries sdr_sk (same hash as dim_users.user_sk for interchangeability) plus active_from / active_to from the dim_users_snapshot SCD-2 history. (source: "Creating a Data Model w/ dbt: Facts", Data Ops notebook.)

sdr_sk.name: sdr_sk
sdr_sk.description: Surrogate key over user_id. Same hash as dim_users.user_sk — interchangeable for joins.

role.name: role
role.description: Always 'SDR' for rows in this dim (filtered at source).

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.description: Always TRUE for rows in this dim (filtered at source).

active_from.name: active_from
active_from.description: UTC timestamp when this SDR's current snapshot row became active. From dim_users_snapshot open row.

active_to.name: active_to
active_to.description: UTC timestamp when the snapshot row was superseded. NULL on the open (current) row.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_sdr.raw_code:
-- SDR-role-filtered conformed dimension on dim_users. Not a duplicate — a
-- subset view. Conformed across fct_speed_to_lead_touch and any future fact
-- that needs SDR-grain filtering (e.g., fct_coaching_events). The rename
-- of user_sk → sdr_sk is for business-readability in the mart; the hash is
-- identical so the two are interchangeable for join purposes.
-- (source: "Creating a Data Model w/ dbt: Facts", Data Ops notebook —
-- "You can still use the same dimensions... it's very common, it's called a
-- conformed dimension.")
--
-- SCD-2 note: active_from / active_to columns pass through from
-- dim_users_snapshot via a separate join pattern. For F1, current-state only
-- (is_active = true). F2 will layer in the snapshot as-of join for
-- role-at-touch-time accuracy.

with

users as (

    select
        user_sk,
        user_id,
        name,
        email,
        role,
        is_active
    from {{ ref('dim_users') }}
    where lower(role) = 'sdr'
      and is_active = true

),

snapshot_history as (

    select
        user_id,
        dbt_valid_from                                          as active_from,
        dbt_valid_to                                            as active_to
    from {{ ref('dim_users_snapshot') }}

),

-- Join to snapshot to surface active_from / active_to from SCD-2 history.
-- For current-row SDRs, take the open snapshot row (dbt_valid_to IS NULL).
latest_snapshot as (

    select
        user_id,
        active_from,
        active_to
    from snapshot_history
    where active_to is null

),

final as (

    select
        -- sdr_sk uses same generate_surrogate_key as dim_users.user_sk
        -- (both hash over user_id) so the keys are interchangeable
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['users.user_id']) }}
                                                                as sdr_sk,

        users.user_sk,
        users.user_id,
        users.name                                              as sdr_name,
        users.email,
        users.role,
        users.is_active,

        ls.active_from,
        ls.active_to

    from users
    left join latest_snapshot ls
        on ls.user_id = users.user_id

)

select * from final
model.dee_data_ops.dim_sdr.compiled_code:

-- conformed dimension.")
--
-- SCD-2 note: active_from / active_to columns pass through from
-- dim_users_snapshot via a separate join pattern. For F1, current-state only
-- (is_active = true). F2 will layer in the snapshot as-of join for
-- role-at-touch-time accuracy.

with

users as (

    select
        user_sk,
        user_id,
        name,
        email,
        role,
        is_active
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_users`
    where lower(role) = 'sdr'
      and is_active = true

),

snapshot_history as (

    select
        user_id,
        dbt_valid_from                                          as active_from,
        dbt_valid_to                                            as active_to
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`snapshots`.`dim_users_snapshot`

),

-- Join to snapshot to surface active_from / active_to from SCD-2 history.
-- For current-row SDRs, take the open snapshot row (dbt_valid_to IS NULL).
latest_snapshot as (

    select
        user_id,
        active_from,
        active_to
    from snapshot_history

),

        -- sdr_sk uses same generate_surrogate_key as dim_users.user_sk

        to_hex(md5(cast(coalesce(cast(users.user_id as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as string)))
                                                                as sdr_sk,

        users.user_sk,
        users.user_id,
        users.name                                              as sdr_name,
        users.email,
        users.role,
        users.is_active,

        ls.active_from,
        ls.active_to

    from users
    left join latest_snapshot ls
        on ls.user_id = users.user_id

)

model.dee_data_ops.dim_offers.name: dim_offers
model.dee_data_ops.dim_offers.description: D-DEE v1 offer stack. Hand-coded inline (not a seed) because the list is tiny and ships with the repo, not a source-system export. One row per SKU. Extend this model when the offer list changes.

offer_name.name: offer_name
offer_name.description: Display name shown in marts / dashboards.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_offers.raw_code:
-- D-DEE v1 offer stack. Hand-coded because the offer list is a business
-- artifact (not a source-system table) and the set is small enough that a
-- full seed file + seed-config coordination is unnecessary overhead. Update
-- this model when D-DEE changes the offer stack; downstream facts join on
-- offer_sk via offer_id.

with

offers as (

    select 'OFFER_DDEE_CORE'       as offer_id,
           'D-DEE Core'             as offer_name,
           'coaching'               as product_type,
           'high_ticket'            as offer_tier,
           true                     as is_active
    union all
    select 'OFFER_DDEE_PAYMENT_PLAN',
           'D-DEE Core (Payment Plan)',
           'coaching',
           'high_ticket',
           true

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['offer_id']) }}  as offer_sk,

        offer_id,
        offer_name,
        product_type,
        offer_tier,
        is_active

    from offers

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.dim_pipeline_stages.name: dim_pipeline_stages
model.dee_data_ops.dim_pipeline_stages.description:
One row per (pipeline, stage) pair — flattens the nested `stages` array from `stg_ghl__pipelines` into a flat dim. Carries the metric-critical `is_booked_stage` boolean so downstream facts and marts can surface the GHL-native "booked" attribute on any row without re-deriving it. See `_dimensions__docs.md` for the is_booked_stage rule set.
**Grain note:** `is_booked_stage` is a dim *attribute* — a downstream descriptor of what a pipeline stage means. It is NOT the grain of the Speed-to-Lead denominator. The denominator grain is Calendly event (one row per booking in `fct_calls_booked`); `dim_pipeline_stages` sits alongside that fact, not replacing it. See `CLAUDE.local.md` "Locked metric" table for the full rationale.

pipeline_stage_sk.name: pipeline_stage_sk
pipeline_stage_sk.description: Surrogate key over `(pipeline_id, stage_id)`. Use this for fact-to-dim joins; `pipeline_id`/`stage_id` are retained for traceability.

pipeline_id.name: pipeline_id
pipeline_id.description: FK to `stg_ghl__pipelines.pipeline_id`. GHL pipeline the stage belongs to.

pipeline_name.name: pipeline_name
pipeline_name.description: Display name of the pipeline (denormalized onto every stage row).

stage_id.name: stage_id
stage_id.description: GHL's native stage id (the value `opportunities.pipelineStageId` points at). Unique within a pipeline.

stage_position.name: stage_position
stage_position.description: Integer ordering of this stage within its pipeline (0-indexed, per GHL).

is_booked_stage.name: is_booked_stage
is_booked_stage.description: True if this stage represents a booked-call state in GHL. Rule: `stage_name` contains `booked` (case-insensitive) OR `stage_name IN ('Set', 'Set/Triage', 'Call Booked', 'Booked Call')`. Rule validated against the oracle Revenue-by-Stage tab (2026-04-20 corpus audit). This is a GHL-native attribute surfaced on rows — not the Speed-to-Lead metric denominator grain (that is Calendly).

show_in_funnel.name: show_in_funnel
show_in_funnel.description: Denormalized from the parent pipeline — whether the owning pipeline renders in GHL's funnel UI. Kept at stage grain so downstream filters don't have to rejoin pipelines.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_pipeline_stages.raw_code:
with pipelines as (

    select
        pipeline_id,
        pipeline_name,
        show_in_funnel,
        stages_json
    from {{ ref('stg_ghl__pipelines') }}

),

stages_unnested as (

    select
        pipeline_id,
        pipeline_name,
        show_in_funnel,
        stage_json
    from pipelines,
    unnest(json_query_array(stages_json)) as stage_json

),

parsed as (

    select
        pipeline_id,
        pipeline_name,
        show_in_funnel,

        json_value(stage_json, '$.id')                          as stage_id,
        json_value(stage_json, '$.name')                        as stage_name,
        cast(json_value(stage_json, '$.position') as int64)     as stage_position

    from stages_unnested

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['pipeline_id', 'stage_id']) }}
                                                                as pipeline_stage_sk,

        pipeline_id,
        pipeline_name,
        stage_id,
        stage_name,
        stage_position,

        case
            when lower(stage_name) like '%booked%'                              then true
            when stage_name in ('Set', 'Set/Triage', 'Call Booked', 'Booked Call') then true
            else false
        end                                                     as is_booked_stage,

        show_in_funnel

    from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.dim_users.name: dim_users
model.dee_data_ops.dim_users.description: Unified users dim — supersedes the previously-planned split `dim_sdrs` + `dim_aes`. From `stg_ghl__users` left-joined with `ghl_sdr_roster` to attach the human-maintained `role` column. Per Track D's grain-audit decision, SDR filtering moves to the mart layer, so this dim is reusable for any downstream cut (SDR / AE / Closer). Dual-role history is tracked via `dim_users_snapshot` (SCD2, check strategy on `role` + `email`).

user_sk.name: user_sk
user_sk.description: Surrogate key over `user_id`. FK target in fct_outreach.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description: Native GHL user id. Natural key. Joins to the roster seed.

role.name: role
role.description: `sdr`, `ae`, or `unknown`. Source: `ghl_sdr_roster.csv`. Users not in the roster default to `unknown`. Role history preserved by `dim_users_snapshot`.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.description: `not is_deleted` from GHL. True when the user currently exists in the GHL users endpoint and is not soft-deleted.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_users.raw_code:
with

ghl_users as (

    select * from {{ ref('stg_ghl__users') }}

),

roster as (

    select * from {{ ref('ghl_sdr_roster') }}

),

joined as (

    select
        ghl_users.user_id,

        coalesce(
            ghl_users.full_name,
            concat(
                coalesce(ghl_users.first_name, ''),
                case
                    when ghl_users.last_name is not null then ' '
                    else ''
                end,
                coalesce(ghl_users.last_name, '')
            )
        )                                                    as name,

        ghl_users.first_name,
        ghl_users.last_name,
        ghl_users.email,
        ghl_users.phone,

        ghl_users.ghl_account_type,
        ghl_users.ghl_role,

        coalesce(roster.role, 'unknown')                     as role,

        not coalesce(ghl_users.is_deleted, false)            as is_active,

        ghl_users._ingested_at

    from ghl_users
    left join roster
        on ghl_users.user_id = roster.user_id

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['user_id']) }}  as user_sk,

        user_id,
        name,
        first_name,
        last_name,
        email,
        phone,

        ghl_account_type,
        ghl_role,
        role,

        is_active,

        _ingested_at

    from joined

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.dim_source.name: dim_source
model.dee_data_ops.dim_source.description: Lead-source conformed dimension. One row per distinct lead_source value from dim_contacts plus an __unknown__ sentinel row for NULL lead_sources. Enriched with human-readable descriptions and is_paid / is_organic flags from the stl_lead_source_lookup seed. Note: actual lead_source values are campaign/content labels (e.g. "ig blueprint case study"), not a controlled channel taxonomy. Seed covers the inferrable channel-level values; campaign- specific labels emit is_paid = NULL and are flagged for David's review.

source_sk.name: source_sk
source_sk.description: Surrogate key over lead_source. PK. Always populated — __unknown__ row handles NULL inputs.

lead_source.name: lead_source
lead_source.description: Raw lead_source value as captured in GHL contacts. __unknown__ for NULL values.

source_description.name: source_description
source_description.description: Human-readable description from stl_lead_source_lookup seed. NULL for unseeded campaign labels.

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.description: TRUE = paid traffic; FALSE = organic/referral. NULL when classification requires David's input.

is_organic.name: is_organic
is_organic.description: Derived as NOT is_paid when is_paid is known; NULL when is_paid is NULL.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_source.raw_code:
-- Lead-source conformed dimension. Surfaces all distinct lead_source values
-- from dim_contacts and enriches them with human-readable descriptions and
-- the is_paid / is_organic flags from the stl_lead_source_lookup seed.
--
-- NOTE: The actual lead_source values in dim_contacts are campaign / content
-- labels (e.g. "ig blueprint case study", "AI Brand Prompts", "dbb-ig") —
-- not the channel-taxonomy values the seed template assumed (google_ads, etc.).
-- The seed covers the inferrable high-volume channel-level values (TikTok,
-- Instagram, YouTube, email, outbound, skool). Campaign-specific labels that
-- cannot be reliably classified as paid/organic without business context emit
-- is_paid = NULL and are flagged in WORKLOG "Open threads" for David.
--
-- The __unknown__ row is emitted unconditionally so that fct_speed_to_lead_touch
-- rows with NULL lead_source always resolve to a non-NULL source_sk.
-- (source: "The Struggle of Data Modeling (Facts vs Dimensions)", Data Ops
-- notebook — descriptive string values belong in the dimension, not the fact.)

with

-- All distinct lead_source values from the contacts dim (including NULL)
all_sources as (

    select distinct lead_source
    from {{ ref('dim_contacts') }}

),

-- Seed with human descriptions + is_paid flag for known values
seed as (

    select
        lead_source,
        source_description,
        cast(is_paid as bool)           as is_paid
    from {{ ref('stl_lead_source_lookup') }}

),

-- The explicit __unknown__ sentinel row for NULL lead_sources
unknown_sentinel as (

    select
        '__unknown__'                   as lead_source,
        'No lead-source attribution captured' as source_description,
        cast(null as bool)              as is_paid

),

-- Coalesce NULL lead_source values from dim_contacts to __unknown__
coalesced_sources as (

    select coalesce(lead_source, '__unknown__') as lead_source
    from all_sources

),

-- Union in the sentinel so __unknown__ is always present
all_sources_with_sentinel as (

    select lead_source from coalesced_sources
    union distinct
    select lead_source from unknown_sentinel

),

-- Left-join the seed enrichment; unseeded values get NULL description / is_paid
enriched as (

    select
        a.lead_source,
        coalesce(s.source_description, u.source_description) as source_description,
        coalesce(s.is_paid,            u.is_paid)            as is_paid
    from all_sources_with_sentinel a
    left join seed s          on lower(s.lead_source) = lower(a.lead_source)
    left join unknown_sentinel u on a.lead_source = '__unknown__'

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['lead_source']) }}
                                        as source_sk,

        lead_source,
        source_description,
        is_paid,

        -- is_organic: derived when is_paid is known; NULL when unknown
        case
            when is_paid is null then cast(null as bool)
            else not is_paid
        end                             as is_organic

    from enriched

)

select * from final

),

final as (

model.dee_data_ops.dim_calendar_dates.name: dim_calendar_dates
model.dee_data_ops.dim_calendar_dates.description: Standard calendar dimension from `dbt_utils.date_spine(start_date='2024-01-01', end_date=current_date+1)`. One row per day with day/week/month/quarter/year enrichment for date-bucketed mart joins and Looker Studio filters.
model.dee_data_ops.dim_calendar_dates.raw_code:
with

date_spine as (

    {{ dbt_utils.date_spine(
        datepart="day",
        start_date="cast('2024-01-01' as date)",
        end_date="date_add(current_date(), interval 1 day)"
    ) }}

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['date_day']) }}   as date_sk,

        cast(date_day as date)                                 as date_day,

        extract(year      from date_day)                      as year_number,
        extract(quarter   from date_day)                      as quarter_number,
        extract(month     from date_day)                      as month_number,
        extract(week      from date_day)                      as week_number,
        extract(day       from date_day)                      as day_of_month,
        extract(dayofweek from date_day)                      as day_of_week,
        extract(dayofyear from date_day)                      as day_of_year,

        format_date('%A',    date_day)                        as day_name,
        format_date('%B',    date_day)                        as month_name,
        format_date('%Y-%m', date_day)                        as year_month,
        format_date('%Y-Q%Q', date_day)                       as year_quarter,

        date_trunc(date_day, week(monday))                    as week_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, month)                           as month_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, quarter)                         as quarter_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, year)                            as year_start_date,

        case
            when extract(dayofweek from date_day) in (1, 7) then false
            else true
        end                                                   as is_weekday

    from date_spine

)

model.dee_data_ops.dim_calendar_dates.compiled_code:
with

date_spine as (

    





with rawdata as (

    

    

    with p as (
        select 0 as generated_number union all select 1
    ), unioned as (

    select

    
    p0.generated_number * power(2, 0)
     + 
    
    p1.generated_number * power(2, 1)
     + 
    
    p2.generated_number * power(2, 2)
     + 
    
    p3.generated_number * power(2, 3)
     + 
    
    p4.generated_number * power(2, 4)
     + 
    
    p5.generated_number * power(2, 5)
     + 
    
    p6.generated_number * power(2, 6)
     + 
    
    p7.generated_number * power(2, 7)
     + 
    
    p8.generated_number * power(2, 8)
     + 
    
    p9.generated_number * power(2, 9)
    
    
    + 1
    as generated_number

    from

    
    p as p0
     cross join 
    
    p as p1
     cross join 
    
    p as p2
     cross join 
    
    p as p3
     cross join 
    
    p as p4
     cross join 
    
    p as p5
     cross join 
    
    p as p6
     cross join 
    
    p as p7
     cross join 
    
    p as p8
     cross join 
    
    p as p9
    
    

    )

    select *
    from unioned
    where generated_number <= 850
    order by generated_number



),

all_periods as (

    select (
        

        datetime_add(
            cast( cast('2024-01-01' as date) as datetime),
        interval row_number() over (order by generated_number) - 1 day
        )


    ) as date_day
    from rawdata

),

filtered as (

    select *
    from all_periods
    where date_day <= date_add(current_date(), interval 1 day)

)

select * from filtered



),

final as (

    select
        to_hex(md5(cast(coalesce(cast(date_day as string), '_dbt_utils_surrogate_key_null_') as string)))   as date_sk,

        cast(date_day as date)                                 as date_day,

        extract(year      from date_day)                      as year_number,
        extract(quarter   from date_day)                      as quarter_number,
        extract(month     from date_day)                      as month_number,
        extract(week      from date_day)                      as week_number,
        extract(day       from date_day)                      as day_of_month,
        extract(dayofweek from date_day)                      as day_of_week,
        extract(dayofyear from date_day)                      as day_of_year,

        format_date('%A',    date_day)                        as day_name,
        format_date('%B',    date_day)                        as month_name,
        format_date('%Y-%m', date_day)                        as year_month,
        format_date('%Y-Q%Q', date_day)                       as year_quarter,

        date_trunc(date_day, week(monday))                    as week_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, month)                           as month_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, quarter)                         as quarter_start_date,
        date_trunc(date_day, year)                            as year_start_date,

        case
            when extract(dayofweek from date_day) in (1, 7) then false
            else true
        end                                                   as is_weekday

    from date_spine

)

model.dee_data_ops.dim_contacts.name: dim_contacts
model.dee_data_ops.dim_contacts.description: Golden-contact dimension anchored on GHL (`stg_ghl__contacts`). Surrogate key over `(location_id, contact_id)`; carries the `attribution_era` bucket (`utm` / `pre_utm`) that downstream marts use to gate era-aware joins, plus placeholder psychographic columns reserved for the Typeform-answers pivot model when it ships. One row per GHL contact — the v1 identity spine. Per `.claude/rules/ warehouse.md`, multi-source bridges land upstream of the dim, not by widening the PK.

contact_sk.name: contact_sk
contact_sk.description: Surrogate key over `(location_id, contact_id)`. FK target.

contact_id.name: contact_id
contact_id.description: Native GHL contact id. Natural key.

location_id.name: location_id
location_id.description: GHL location id. Composite with `contact_id` in the SK.

email_norm.name: email_norm
email_norm.description: Lowercased / trimmed email — canonical join key for bridges.

attribution_era.name: attribution_era
attribution_era.description: `utm` when `lead_source` matches a paid/tracked pattern; otherwise `pre_utm`. Per-contact, not per-campaign — drives era-aware mart joins.

has_typeform_utm.name: has_typeform_utm
has_typeform_utm.description: True when the Typeform-join yielded a response carrying any `hidden_utm_*` value. Placeholder today (Typeform email-join bridge still owed); shape preserved for drop-in.

model.dee_data_ops.dim_contacts.raw_code:
with

ghl_contacts as (

    select * from {{ ref('stg_ghl__contacts') }}

),

typeform_responses as (

    select * from {{ ref('stg_typeform__responses') }}

),

-- Collapse typeform to one row per email, keeping latest submission signals
-- and whether any UTM attribution is present. Aggregated here (pre-join) so
-- the dim stays flat and never fans contacts out.
typeform_enrichment as (

    select
        lower(trim(typeform_responses.response_token))       as response_token,
        max(typeform_responses.submitted_at)                 as latest_typeform_at,

        max(typeform_responses.hidden_utm_source)            as utm_source,
        max(typeform_responses.hidden_utm_medium)            as utm_medium,
        max(typeform_responses.hidden_utm_campaign)          as utm_campaign,

        max(typeform_responses.calculated_score)             as psychographic_score,

        max(
            case
                when typeform_responses.hidden_utm_source   is not null
                  or typeform_responses.hidden_utm_medium   is not null
                  or typeform_responses.hidden_utm_campaign is not null
                then 1 else 0
            end
        )                                                    as has_utm_int

    from typeform_responses
    group by 1

),

-- GHL contacts hold no email on the Typeform join axis natively (Typeform
-- exposes no email in `stg_typeform__responses`); we carry attribution on a
-- best-effort basis via the landing-token EAV join owed in the downstream
-- bridge layer. For v1 the per-contact `attribution_era` derives from
-- `lead_source` rather than Typeform join, and psychographic enrichment is
-- left null-safe until the Typeform-answers pivot model ships.
joined as (

    select
        ghl_contacts.contact_id,
        ghl_contacts.location_id,
        ghl_contacts.assigned_user_id,
        ghl_contacts.business_id,

        ghl_contacts.contact_name,
        ghl_contacts.first_name,
        ghl_contacts.last_name,
        ghl_contacts.company_name,

        ghl_contacts.email,
        lower(trim(ghl_contacts.email))                      as email_norm,
        ghl_contacts.phone,

        ghl_contacts.contact_type,
        ghl_contacts.lead_source,
        ghl_contacts.is_dnd,

        ghl_contacts.city,
        ghl_contacts.state,
        ghl_contacts.postal_code,
        ghl_contacts.address1,
        ghl_contacts.country,
        ghl_contacts.timezone,
        ghl_contacts.website,
        ghl_contacts.date_of_birth,

        ghl_contacts.contact_created_at,
        ghl_contacts.contact_updated_at,

        -- attribution_era: UTM vs pre-UTM, per-contact. Derived from
        -- `lead_source` because the Typeform-to-contact email-join bridge
        -- is not yet in staging. Contacts whose lead_source is non-null
        -- and matches a paid/tracked pattern → 'utm'; everything else
        -- → 'pre_utm'. Downstream era-aware mart joins use this column.
        case
            when lower(ghl_contacts.lead_source) in (
                'paid-ads', 'google-ads', 'facebook-ads',
                'paid_ads', 'google_ads', 'facebook_ads',
                'paid ads', 'google ads', 'facebook ads',
                'paid', 'google', 'facebook', 'meta', 'tiktok'
            )
            then 'utm'
            when ghl_contacts.lead_source is null
            then 'pre_utm'
            else 'pre_utm'
        end                                                  as attribution_era,

        typeform_enrichment.latest_typeform_at,
        typeform_enrichment.utm_source,
        typeform_enrichment.utm_medium,
        typeform_enrichment.utm_campaign,
        typeform_enrichment.psychographic_score,
        coalesce(typeform_enrichment.has_utm_int, 0) = 1     as has_typeform_utm,

        ghl_contacts._ingested_at

    from ghl_contacts
    -- Typeform join intentionally left as a no-op placeholder until the
    -- email/landing-token bridge ships. `response_token` is not GHL's
    -- contact identifier; this left join yields all nulls today but keeps
    -- the column contract stable for the future bridge drop-in.
    left join typeform_enrichment
        on lower(trim(ghl_contacts.email)) = typeform_enrichment.response_token

),

final as (

    select
        {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(
            ['location_id', 'contact_id']
        ) }}                                                 as contact_sk,

        contact_id,
        location_id,
        assigned_user_id,
        business_id,

        contact_name,
        first_name,
        last_name,
        company_name,

        email,
        email_norm,
        phone,

        contact_type,
        lead_source,
        is_dnd,

        city,
        state,
        postal_code,
        address1,
        country,
        timezone,
        website,
        date_of_birth,

        attribution_era,

        latest_typeform_at,
        utm_source,
        utm_medium,
        utm_campaign,
        psychographic_score,
        has_typeform_utm,

        contact_created_at,
        contact_updated_at,
        _ingested_at

    from joined

)

select * from final
