model.dee_data_ops.speed_to_lead_detail.name: speed_to_lead_detail
model.dee_data_ops.speed_to_lead_detail.description:
Wide mart for the Speed-to-Lead domain. Grain: one row per (booking × touch-event).
Bookings with no SDR touch emit ONE row with touched_at = NULL.
Downstream consumers: Metabase Speed-to-Lead dashboard (all cards on Page 1 after F2 rewire).
Sibling mart `sales_activity_detail` is preserved for Page 2/Page 3 dashboards.
Column order is frozen as the BI-layer contract — new columns added at the END only.
Source: "How to Create a Data Modeling Pipeline (3 Layer Approach)", Data Ops notebook.

speed_to_lead_touch_id.name: speed_to_lead_touch_id
speed_to_lead_touch_id.description: Surrogate PK. Hash of (booking_sk, coalesce(touch_sk, 'no-touch')). Mart-layer rename of fct_speed_to_lead_touch.speed_to_lead_touch_sk.

booking_id.name: booking_id
booking_id.description: Surrogate FK to fct_calls_booked. Mart-layer rename of booking_sk.

contact_id.name: contact_id
contact_id.description: GHL contact_id. NULL if contact row missing (pre-attribution era data gap).

booked_at.name: booked_at
booked_at.description: Timestamp when the Calendly booking was created. Partition key.

scheduled_for.name: scheduled_for
scheduled_for.description: Scheduled start time of the call. NULL if not available.

booked_date.name: booked_date
booked_date.description: Date portion of booked_at. Field-Filter-friendly for Metabase date filters.

cancelled_at.name: cancelled_at
cancelled_at.description: Timestamp of cancellation. NULL if not cancelled.

sdr_name.name: sdr_name
sdr_name.description: Name of the SDR who placed the first outbound touch. NULL for no-touch rows.

sdr_email.name: sdr_email
sdr_email.description: Email of the SDR. NULL for no-touch rows.

touched_at.name: touched_at
touched_at.description: Timestamp of the touch event. NULL for no-touch rows.

channel.name: channel
channel.description: Touch channel (CALL, SMS, etc.). NULL for no-touch rows.

minutes_to_touch.name: minutes_to_touch
minutes_to_touch.description: Minutes between booked_at and touched_at. NULL for no-touch rows.

is_first_touch.name: is_first_touch
is_first_touch.description: TRUE on the OVERALL earliest outbound touch row per booking, regardless of toucher role. FALSE for subsequent touches and no-touch rows. Pair with `is_sdr_touch` to scope to "first SDR toucher" (reproduces the legacy 2026-04-19 locked metric semantics).

is_within_5_min_sla.name: is_within_5_min_sla
is_within_5_min_sla.description: TRUE when is_first_touch=TRUE AND is_sdr_touch=TRUE AND minutes_to_touch < 5. The headline Speed-to-Lead numerator.

had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr.name: had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr
had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr.description: TRUE if any SDR activity occurred within 60 minutes of booked_at, regardless of touch order.

is_sdr_touch.name: is_sdr_touch
is_sdr_touch.description: TRUE when the touching user's role = 'SDR'. FALSE for non-SDR or NULL toucher.

lead_source.name: lead_source
lead_source.description: Lead source label from dim_source. NULL entries resolve to '__unknown__' via dim_source sentinel row.

source_description.name: source_description
source_description.description: Human-readable description of the lead source from stl_lead_source_lookup seed.

is_paid_source.name: is_paid_source
is_paid_source.description: TRUE = paid channel, FALSE = organic, NULL = unclassified (approx 88% of rows — campaign labels not yet seeded). See WORKLOG 2026-04-22 for F3 classification plan.

show_outcome.name: show_outcome
show_outcome.description: v1 heuristic from Calendly event_status + GHL stage-change signal. Values: showed / no_show / cancelled / pending. See fct_speed_to_lead_touch header comment for derivation detail.

close_outcome.name: close_outcome
close_outcome.description: won / lost / pending based on GHL opportunity status.

closed_at.name: closed_at
closed_at.description: Timestamp when opportunity was won or lost. NULL if still pending.

cycle_time_booking_to_close_min.name: cycle_time_booking_to_close_min
cycle_time_booking_to_close_min.description: Minutes from booked_at to closed_at for won opportunities.

lost_reason.name: lost_reason
lost_reason.description: GHL lost_reason_id. NULL if not lost.

attribution_quality_flag.name: attribution_quality_flag
attribution_quality_flag.description: Data quality tier: clean / no_sdr_touch / pre_utm_era / role_unknown.

attribution_era.name: attribution_era
attribution_era.description: UTM coverage era for the contact. Values from dim_contacts.

era_flag.name: era_flag
era_flag.description: ramping (booked_at < 2026-03-16) or stable. Used to exclude the ramp period from trend analysis.

pipeline_name.name: pipeline_name
pipeline_name.description: GHL pipeline name. NULL in dev (fct_calls_booked.pipeline_stage_sk is stubbed NULL until GHL stage-sync ships).

stage_name.name: stage_name
stage_name.description: GHL pipeline stage name. NULL in dev.

is_booked_stage.name: is_booked_stage
is_booked_stage.description: TRUE when the pipeline stage is a booking-confirmation stage. NULL in dev.

days_since_stage_change.name: days_since_stage_change
days_since_stage_change.description: Days since the last GHL stage change. Always NULL (booking-level signal not available on dim_pipeline_stages; reserved for a future fact-level computation).

first_touch_campaign.name: first_touch_campaign
first_touch_campaign.description: UTM campaign of the first marketing touch. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

first_touch_source.name: first_touch_source
first_touch_source.description: UTM source of the first marketing touch. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

first_touch_medium.name: first_touch_medium
first_touch_medium.description: UTM medium of the first marketing touch. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

last_touch_campaign.name: last_touch_campaign
last_touch_campaign.description: UTM campaign of the last marketing touch before booking. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

last_touch_source.name: last_touch_source
last_touch_source.description: UTM source of the last marketing touch. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

last_touch_medium.name: last_touch_medium
last_touch_medium.description: UTM medium of the last marketing touch. NULL until Track E invitee staging ships.

client.name: client
client.description: Tenant identifier. Hard-coded 'Dee' for v1 single-tenant. Widen when multi-tenant ships.

mart_refreshed_at.name: mart_refreshed_at
mart_refreshed_at.description: Timestamp when this mart row was last built (current_timestamp() at dbt build time).

model.dee_data_ops.speed_to_lead_detail.raw_code:
{{
    config(
        materialized='table',
        partition_by={
            'field': 'booked_at',
            'data_type': 'timestamp',
            'granularity': 'day'
        }
    )
}}

-- Wide mart for the Speed-to-Lead domain.
-- Grain: one row per (booking × touch-event). Bookings with zero outbound
-- human SDR touches emit ONE row with touched_at = NULL (no-touch rows).
-- Downstream consumers: Metabase `Speed-to-Lead` dashboard (via native-SQL
-- aggregations). Sibling mart `sales_activity_detail` is kept for Page 2/Page 3
-- dashboards pending their own refactors. Built on `fct_speed_to_lead_touch`
-- to preserve lowest-grain show_outcome column — see that fact's header comment.
-- Column order is the BI-layer contract — new columns go at the END of the list.
-- Source: "How to Create a Data Modeling Pipeline (3 Layer Approach)",
-- Data Ops notebook.

with

fact as (

    select
        speed_to_lead_touch_sk,
        booking_sk,
        touch_sk,
        contact_sk,
        sdr_sk,
        source_sk,
        booked_at,
        touched_at,
        minutes_to_touch,
        is_first_touch,
        is_within_5_min_sla,
        had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,
        is_sdr_touch,
        channel,
        event_status,
        cancelled_at,
        show_outcome,
        close_outcome,
        closed_at,
        lost_reason,
        attribution_quality_flag,
        attribution_era,
        era_flag,
        mart_refreshed_at
    from {{ ref('fct_speed_to_lead_touch') }}

),

sdr as (

    select
        sdr_sk,
        sdr_name,
        email                               as sdr_email
    from {{ ref('dim_sdr') }}

),

src as (

    select
        source_sk,
        lead_source,
        source_description,
        is_paid                             as is_paid_source
    from {{ ref('dim_source') }}

),

contacts as (

    select
        contact_sk,
        contact_id,
        email,
        contact_name                        as full_name,
        -- Multi-touch attribution stubs (NULL until Track E invitee staging ships)
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_campaign,
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_source,
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_medium,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_campaign,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_source,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_medium
    from {{ ref('dim_contacts') }}

),

-- fct_calls_booked carries scheduled_for + pipeline_stage_sk FK
bookings as (

    select
        booking_sk,
        scheduled_for,
        pipeline_stage_sk
    from {{ ref('fct_calls_booked') }}

),

final as (

    select
        -- PK (business-friendly rename at mart layer per .claude/rules/mart-naming.md)
        fact.speed_to_lead_touch_sk         as speed_to_lead_touch_id,
        fact.booking_sk                     as booking_id,
        cont.contact_id,
        cont.email,
        cont.full_name,

        -- Booking timing
        fact.booked_at,
        bk.scheduled_for,
        date(fact.booked_at)                as booked_date,
        fact.event_status,
        fact.cancelled_at,

        -- SDR identity
        sdr.sdr_name,
        sdr.sdr_email,

        -- Touch metrics
        fact.touched_at,
        fact.channel,
        fact.minutes_to_touch,
        fact.is_first_touch,
        fact.is_within_5_min_sla,
        fact.had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,
        fact.is_sdr_touch,

        -- Lead source
        src.lead_source,
        src.source_description,
        src.is_paid_source,

        -- Outcome
        fact.show_outcome,
        fact.close_outcome,
        fact.closed_at,
        case
            when fact.close_outcome = 'won'
                then timestamp_diff(fact.closed_at, fact.booked_at, minute)
        end                                 as cycle_time_booking_to_close_min,
        fact.lost_reason,

        -- Attribution
        fact.attribution_quality_flag,
        fact.attribution_era,
        fact.era_flag,

        -- Pipeline state at booking time. fct_calls_booked.pipeline_stage_sk
        -- resolves via the most-recent GHL opp whose
        -- `opportunity_created_at <= booked_at`; NULL when the contact has no
        -- pre-booking opportunity (see fct_calls_booked.sql header).
        ps.pipeline_name,
        ps.stage_name,
        ps.is_booked_stage,
        cast(null as int64)                 as days_since_stage_change,

        -- Multi-touch attribution stubs (NULL until Track E invitee staging ships)
        cont.first_touch_campaign,
        cont.first_touch_source,
        cont.first_touch_medium,
        cont.last_touch_campaign,
        cont.last_touch_source,
        cont.last_touch_medium,

        -- Tenant
        cast('Dee' as string)               as client,

        fact.mart_refreshed_at

    from fact
    left join contacts cont
        on cont.contact_sk = fact.contact_sk
    left join sdr
        on sdr.sdr_sk = fact.sdr_sk
    left join src
        on src.source_sk = fact.source_sk
    left join bookings bk
        on bk.booking_sk = fact.booking_sk
    left join {{ ref('dim_pipeline_stages') }} ps
        on ps.pipeline_stage_sk = bk.pipeline_stage_sk

)

select * from final
model.dee_data_ops.speed_to_lead_detail.compiled_code:
-- Wide mart for the Speed-to-Lead domain.
-- Grain: one row per (booking × touch-event). Bookings with zero outbound
-- human SDR touches emit ONE row with touched_at = NULL (no-touch rows).
-- Downstream consumers: Metabase `Speed-to-Lead` dashboard (via native-SQL
-- aggregations). Sibling mart `sales_activity_detail` is kept for Page 2/Page 3
-- dashboards pending their own refactors. Built on `fct_speed_to_lead_touch`
-- to preserve lowest-grain show_outcome column — see that fact's header comment.
-- Column order is the BI-layer contract — new columns go at the END of the list.
-- Source: "How to Create a Data Modeling Pipeline (3 Layer Approach)",
-- Data Ops notebook.

with

fact as (

    select
        speed_to_lead_touch_sk,
        booking_sk,
        touch_sk,
        contact_sk,
        sdr_sk,
        source_sk,
        booked_at,
        touched_at,
        minutes_to_touch,
        is_first_touch,
        is_within_5_min_sla,
        had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,
        is_sdr_touch,
        channel,
        event_status,
        cancelled_at,
        show_outcome,
        close_outcome,
        closed_at,
        lost_reason,
        attribution_quality_flag,
        attribution_era,
        era_flag,
        mart_refreshed_at
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`fct_speed_to_lead_touch`

),

sdr as (

    select
        sdr_sk,
        sdr_name,
        email                               as sdr_email
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_sdr`

),

src as (

    select
        source_sk,
        lead_source,
        source_description,
        is_paid                             as is_paid_source
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_source`

),

contacts as (

    select
        contact_sk,
        contact_id,
        email,
        contact_name                        as full_name,
        -- Multi-touch attribution stubs (NULL until Track E invitee staging ships)
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_campaign,
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_source,
        cast(null as string)                as first_touch_medium,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_campaign,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_source,
        cast(null as string)                as last_touch_medium
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_contacts`

),

-- fct_calls_booked carries scheduled_for + pipeline_stage_sk FK
bookings as (

    select
        booking_sk,
        scheduled_for,
        pipeline_stage_sk
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`fct_calls_booked`

),

final as (

    select
        -- PK (business-friendly rename at mart layer per .claude/rules/mart-naming.md)
        fact.speed_to_lead_touch_sk         as speed_to_lead_touch_id,
        fact.booking_sk                     as booking_id,
        cont.contact_id,
        cont.email,
        cont.full_name,

        -- Booking timing
        fact.booked_at,
        bk.scheduled_for,
        date(fact.booked_at)                as booked_date,
        fact.event_status,
        fact.cancelled_at,

        -- SDR identity
        sdr.sdr_name,
        sdr.sdr_email,

        -- Touch metrics
        fact.touched_at,
        fact.channel,
        fact.minutes_to_touch,
        fact.is_first_touch,
        fact.is_within_5_min_sla,
        fact.had_any_sdr_activity_within_1_hr,
        fact.is_sdr_touch,

        -- Lead source
        src.lead_source,
        src.source_description,
        src.is_paid_source,

        -- Outcome
        fact.show_outcome,
        fact.close_outcome,
        fact.closed_at,
        case
            when fact.close_outcome = 'won'
                then timestamp_diff(fact.closed_at, fact.booked_at, minute)
        end                                 as cycle_time_booking_to_close_min,
        fact.lost_reason,

        -- Attribution
        fact.attribution_quality_flag,
        fact.attribution_era,
        fact.era_flag,

        -- Pipeline state at booking time. fct_calls_booked.pipeline_stage_sk
        -- resolves via the most-recent GHL opp whose
        -- `opportunity_created_at <= booked_at`; NULL when the contact has no
        -- pre-booking opportunity (see fct_calls_booked.sql header).
        ps.pipeline_name,
        ps.stage_name,
        ps.is_booked_stage,
        cast(null as int64)                 as days_since_stage_change,

        -- Multi-touch attribution stubs (NULL until Track E invitee staging ships)
        cont.first_touch_campaign,
        cont.first_touch_source,
        cont.first_touch_medium,
        cont.last_touch_campaign,
        cont.last_touch_source,
        cont.last_touch_medium,

        -- Tenant
        cast('Dee' as string)               as client,

        fact.mart_refreshed_at

    from fact
    left join contacts cont
        on cont.contact_sk = fact.contact_sk
    left join sdr
        on sdr.sdr_sk = fact.sdr_sk
    left join src
        on src.source_sk = fact.source_sk
    left join bookings bk
        on bk.booking_sk = fact.booking_sk
    left join `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_pipeline_stages` ps
        on ps.pipeline_stage_sk = bk.pipeline_stage_sk

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stl_data_freshness.name: stl_data_freshness
model.dee_data_ops.stl_data_freshness.description: One-row rollup feeding the "Data as of" freshness number card on the Speed-to-Lead dashboard. Grain: exactly one row. Exposes the latest booking timestamp on sales_activity_detail and the dbt run timestamp so viewers can tell at a glance how current the dashboard is.

last_booking_at.name: last_booking_at
last_booking_at.description: Max(booked_at) on sales_activity_detail. Represents the latest event the Speed-to-Lead data layer has seen. This is what the "Data as of" freshness tile on the dashboard displays, formatted as a datetime.

run_ts.name: run_ts
run_ts.description: current_timestamp() evaluated at dbt build time. Useful for distinguishing "the mart is stale because no new bookings" vs "the mart is stale because dbt hasn't run recently."

model.dee_data_ops.stl_data_freshness.raw_code:
-- One-row rollup exposing the latest booking timestamp on sales_activity_detail
-- plus the run timestamp of the dbt build. Feeds the "Data as of HH:MM"
-- freshness number card on the Speed-to-Lead dashboard.
--
-- Grain: exactly 1 row. Materialized as a table so Metabase's result cache +
-- BigQuery's native result cache both get a stable identity to key off.
--
-- Why max(booked_at), not current_timestamp(): per Metabase Learn
-- "BI Dashboard Visualization Best Practices" (corpus: Metabase Learn
-- notebook), a freshness card should show "the latest value of a timestamp
-- column that represents the data's natural reporting cadence" — NOT the
-- query execution time. current_timestamp() would be a tautology (it is
-- always "now"). max(booked_at) tells the viewer the latest event the
-- mart has seen, which is what "data is current as of X" really means.
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

select
    max(booked_at)      as last_booking_at,
    current_timestamp() as run_ts
from {{ ref('sales_activity_detail') }}

snapshot.dee_data_ops.dim_users_snapshot.name: dim_users_snapshot
snapshot.dee_data_ops.dim_users_snapshot.description: SCD Type 2 history of `dim_users` using the `check` strategy on `name`, `role`, `email`, and `is_active`. Closed rows carry `dbt_valid_to`; open rows have `dbt_valid_to is null`. Powers mart-level as-of joins where "who did what, under which role, at event time?" matters — essential for the Speed-to-Lead numerator (SDR-at-time-of-touch) and any retroactive role reclassification (e.g. Blagoj dual-role resolution, Ayaan / Jake pending confirmation).

dbt_valid_from.name: dbt_valid_from
dbt_valid_from.description: UTC timestamp this row became the current truth.

dbt_valid_to.name: dbt_valid_to
dbt_valid_to.description: UTC timestamp this row was superseded. NULL on the open row.

snapshot.dee_data_ops.dim_users_snapshot.compiled_code: select user_id, name, role, email, is_active from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_users`

test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_sales_act.name: release_gate_sales_activity_detail
test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_sales_act.raw_code:
{{ config(
    severity = 'warn'
) }}

-- Release gate: fail the build if sales_activity_detail row count deviates
-- more than ±5% from the oracle 'Calls Booked' snapshot (3,141 as of 2026-03-19).
-- Tolerance is deliberately tight — see `client_v1_scope_speed_to_lead.md` for the
-- reasoning behind treating Calendly as the system-of-record for the booking grain.

with

mart as (
    select count(*) as n from {{ ref('sales_activity_detail') }}
),

oracle as (
    select cast(value as int64) as n
    from {{ ref('oracle_dashboard_metrics_20260319') }}
    where metric = 'Calls Booked'
),

delta as (
    select
        mart.n                                              as mart_count,
        oracle.n                                            as oracle_count,
        abs(mart.n - oracle.n) / oracle.n                   as pct_delta
    from mart, oracle
)

select *
from delta
where pct_delta > 0.05

test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_revenue_d.name: release_gate_revenue_detail
test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_revenue_d.raw_code:
{{ config(
    severity = 'warn'
) }}

-- Release-gate test for revenue_detail.
-- Three assertions, any one failure returns rows and fails the test:
--   1. total revenue within 5% of the 2026-03-19 oracle dashboard
--   2. payment-row count within 5% of the oracle total (1423)
--   3. unmatched revenue share stays under 10% — hard stop on silent
--      attribution decay.
-- Parity gaps explained by the known Fanbasis zero-row state should be
-- handled by widening tolerance in the PR, not by filtering rows.

with

mart as (

    select
        sum(net_amount)                                     as mart_revenue,
        count(*)                                            as mart_count,
        sum(
            case
                when match_status = 'unmatched' then net_amount
                else 0
            end
        )                                                   as unmatched_revenue
    from {{ ref('revenue_detail') }}

),

oracle as (

    select
        cast(
            replace(replace(value, '$', ''), ',', '')
            as numeric
        )                                                   as oracle_revenue
    from {{ ref('oracle_dashboard_metrics_20260319') }}
    where metric = 'Total Revenue (USD)'

),

delta as (

    select
        mart.mart_revenue,
        oracle.oracle_revenue,
        abs(mart.mart_revenue - oracle.oracle_revenue)
        / oracle.oracle_revenue                             as revenue_pct_delta,
        mart.mart_count,
        mart.unmatched_revenue / mart.mart_revenue          as unmatched_share
    from mart
    cross join oracle

)

select *
from delta
where revenue_pct_delta > numeric '0.05'
    or mart_count < 1350
    or mart_count > 1494
    or unmatched_share > numeric '0.10'

test.dee_data_ops.bridge_payment_count_p.name: bridge_payment_count_parity
test.dee_data_ops.bridge_payment_count_p.raw_code:
-- Asserts the bridge contains exactly one row per upstream payment.
-- Catches the latent failure where a payment falls through every tier
-- (e.g., phone-only payment with no contact phone match in `dim_contacts`)
-- and is silently dropped from the bridge — which would propagate as a
-- NULL bridge join in `fct_payments` instead of `bridge_status =
-- 'unmatched'`. Per `_bridges__docs.md`: "every payment gets exactly
-- one row" — this test is the enforceable form of that promise.
--
-- Returns one row per `source_platform` whose bridge count differs from
-- staging count. Test passes when zero rows return.

with bridge_counts as (

    select
        source_platform,
        count(*) as bridge_n
    from {{ ref('bridge_identity_contact_payment') }}
    group by 1

),

staging_counts as (

    select
        'stripe'                                                  as source_platform,
        count(*)                                                  as staging_n
    from {{ ref('stg_stripe__charges') }}
    where charge_id is not null

    union all

    select
        'fanbasis'                                                as source_platform,
        count(*)                                                  as staging_n
    from {{ ref('stg_fanbasis__transactions') }}
    where payment_id is not null

),

joined as (

    select
        staging_counts.source_platform,
        staging_counts.staging_n,
        coalesce(bridge_counts.bridge_n, 0)                       as bridge_n,
        staging_counts.staging_n - coalesce(bridge_counts.bridge_n, 0)
                                                                  as missing_from_bridge
    from staging_counts
    left join bridge_counts
        on bridge_counts.source_platform = staging_counts.source_platform

)

select * from joined
where missing_from_bridge != 0

test.dee_data_ops.revenue_detail_refunds.name: revenue_detail_refunds_parity
test.dee_data_ops.revenue_detail_refunds.raw_code:
-- Asserts the refunds aggregation in `revenue_detail` matches the
-- underlying `fct_refunds` total per `source_platform`. Catches:
--   (a) refund-side filter regression in revenue_detail's aggregation CTE
--       (e.g. a stray WHERE that drops a row),
--   (b) join-key drift if either side renames `parent_payment_id` /
--       `payment_id` / `source_platform`,
--   (c) accidental fan-out if the pre-aggregation is removed and the
--       refunds CTE joins payments at refund-grain instead of payment-grain.
--
-- Money columns through fct_refunds and revenue_detail are NUMERIC end-to-end
-- (Fanbasis-only refund path). Tolerance kept at $0.01 as a precision floor
-- in case downstream summation introduces rounding via mixed-type arithmetic;
-- with both sides NUMERIC this is effectively exact.
-- Returns one row per offending source_platform; test passes when zero
-- rows return.

with

mart_refunds as (

    select
        source_platform,
        sum(refunds_total_amount)                       as mart_total
    from {{ ref('revenue_detail') }}
    group by 1

),

fact_refunds as (

    select
        source_platform,
        sum(refund_amount)                              as fact_total
    from {{ ref('fct_refunds') }}
    group by 1

),

mismatches as (

    select
        coalesce(m.source_platform, f.source_platform) as source_platform,
        coalesce(m.mart_total, 0)                      as mart_total,
        coalesce(f.fact_total, 0)                      as fact_total,
        abs(coalesce(m.mart_total, 0) - coalesce(f.fact_total, 0))
                                                       as abs_diff
    from mart_refunds m
    full outer join fact_refunds f
        on m.source_platform = f.source_platform

)

select *
from mismatches
where abs_diff > 0.01

test.dee_data_ops.stl_grain_integrity.name: stl_grain_integrity
test.dee_data_ops.stl_grain_integrity.raw_code:
-- Singular test: (booking_id, touch_key) must be unique in speed_to_lead_detail.
-- touch_key = coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch') so that
-- no-touch rows (touched_at = NULL) are treated as a single unique row per
-- booking, not as a group of duplicates.
--
-- Returns rows on failure (any duplicate combination). Zero rows = grain is clean.

select
    booking_id,
    coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch') as touch_key,
    count(*)                                         as n
from {{ ref('speed_to_lead_detail') }}
group by
    booking_id,
    coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch')
having count(*) > 1
test.dee_data_ops.stl_grain_integrity.compiled_code:
-- Singular test: (booking_id, touch_key) must be unique in speed_to_lead_detail.
-- touch_key = coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch') so that
-- no-touch rows (touched_at = NULL) are treated as a single unique row per
-- booking, not as a group of duplicates.
--
-- Returns rows on failure (any duplicate combination). Zero rows = grain is clean.

select
    booking_id,
    coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch') as touch_key,
    count(*)                                         as n
from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Marts`.`speed_to_lead_detail`
group by
    booking_id,
    coalesce(cast(touched_at as string), 'no-touch')
having count(*) > 1

test.dee_data_ops.fanbasis_refund_parity.name: fanbasis_refund_parity
test.dee_data_ops.fanbasis_refund_parity.raw_code:
-- Asserts the two Fanbasis staging views agree on which transactions
-- have refunds. `stg_fanbasis__transactions.is_refunded` is derived
-- from `array_length($.refunds) > 0`; `stg_fanbasis__refunds` unnests
-- the same array. The set of `payment_id` values where `is_refunded =
-- true` should equal the set of distinct `payment_id` values that
-- appear in the refunds staging.
--
-- Catches: (a) extractor regression where `$.refunds` lands as a
-- JSON-string instead of an array (silent zero-rows in refunds view
-- but `is_refunded` still true), (b) refund-object missing `$.id` so
-- it gets dropped by the staging filter (parent has is_refunded=true
-- but no refund row exists), (c) inverse — refunds staging lands rows
-- whose parent transaction was filtered out.
--
-- Returns one row per offending payment_id; test passes when zero
-- rows return.

with transactions_with_refunds as (

    select payment_id
    from {{ ref('stg_fanbasis__transactions') }}
    where is_refunded = true

),

refunds_parents as (

    select distinct payment_id
    from {{ ref('stg_fanbasis__refunds') }}

),

mismatches as (

    select
        coalesce(t.payment_id, r.payment_id) as payment_id,
        case
            when t.payment_id is null then 'orphan_refund_no_parent'
            when r.payment_id is null then 'parent_marked_refunded_no_event'
        end                                  as failure_mode
    from transactions_with_refunds t
    full outer join refunds_parents r
        on t.payment_id = r.payment_id
    where t.payment_id is null
       or r.payment_id is null

)

select * from mismatches
test.dee_data_ops.fanbasis_refund_parity.compiled_code:
-- Asserts the two Fanbasis staging views agree on which transactions
-- have refunds. `stg_fanbasis__transactions.is_refunded` is derived
-- from `array_length($.refunds) > 0`; `stg_fanbasis__refunds` unnests
-- the same array. The set of `payment_id` values where `is_refunded =
-- true` should equal the set of distinct `payment_id` values that
-- appear in the refunds staging.
--
-- Catches: (a) extractor regression where `$.refunds` lands as a
-- JSON-string instead of an array (silent zero-rows in refunds view
-- but `is_refunded` still true), (b) refund-object missing `$.id` so
-- it gets dropped by the staging filter (parent has is_refunded=true
-- but no refund row exists), (c) inverse — refunds staging lands rows
-- whose parent transaction was filtered out.
--
-- Returns one row per offending payment_id; test passes when zero
-- rows return.

with transactions_with_refunds as (

    select payment_id
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_fanbasis__transactions`
    where is_refunded = true

),

refunds_parents as (

    select distinct payment_id
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_fanbasis__refunds`

),

mismatches as (

    select
        coalesce(t.payment_id, r.payment_id) as payment_id,
        case
            when t.payment_id is null then 'orphan_refund_no_parent'
            when r.payment_id is null then 'parent_marked_refunded_no_event'
        end                                  as failure_mode
    from transactions_with_refunds t
    full outer join refunds_parents r
        on t.payment_id = r.payment_id
    where t.payment_id is null
       or r.payment_id is null

)

select * from mismatches

test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_lead_jour.name: release_gate_lead_journey
test.dee_data_ops.release_gate_lead_jour.raw_code:
{{ config(
    severity = 'warn'
) }}

-- Release-gate parity test for the contact-grain mart.
--
-- Two assertions pulled against the 2026-03-19 oracle seed:
--   1. Row count within ±5% of `Total Leads` (15,598).
--   2. Applicant count within ±10% of `Applications Submitted` (6,113).
--
-- Tolerance rationale: the row count is anchored on `dim_contacts`,
-- which is 1:1 with GHL contacts — a tight ±5% is reasonable. The
-- applicant count depends on Typeform-to-contact join fidelity, so
-- it carries a wider ±10% tolerance.
--
-- Known failing assertion until upstream lands: `application_submitted`
-- is a NULL placeholder until the Typeform-answers pivot ships.
-- `countif(null)` returns 0, so `applicant_pct_delta` will equal 1.0
-- (-100%) and this test will fail on that assertion. Keep the test
-- as-is — it correctly signals that the applicant-join upstream is
-- still owed. Flips green automatically when the upstream ships.

with

mart as (

    select
        count(*)                                                as mart_rows,
        countif(application_submitted)                          as mart_applicants
    from {{ ref('lead_journey') }}

),

oracle as (

    select
        cast((select value from {{ ref('oracle_dashboard_metrics_20260319') }} where metric = 'Total Leads') as int64)
                                                                as oracle_rows,
        cast((select value from {{ ref('oracle_dashboard_metrics_20260319') }} where metric = 'Applications Submitted') as int64)
                                                                as oracle_applicants

),

delta as (

    select
        mart.mart_rows,
        oracle.oracle_rows,
        safe_divide(abs(mart.mart_rows - oracle.oracle_rows), oracle.oracle_rows)
                                                                as row_pct_delta,
        mart.mart_applicants,
        oracle.oracle_applicants,
        safe_divide(abs(mart.mart_applicants - oracle.oracle_applicants), oracle.oracle_applicants)
                                                                as applicant_pct_delta
    from mart, oracle

)

select *
from delta
where row_pct_delta       > 0.05
   or applicant_pct_delta > 0.10

test.dee_data_ops.source_freshness.name: source_freshness
test.dee_data_ops.source_freshness.raw_code:
-- Hard-fail singular test — any declared raw source >25h stale trips dbt test.
-- Complements `dbt source freshness` (warn-only in nightly) with a blocking
-- check that can gate CI / prod deploys.
--
-- Post-U3 (2026-04-23): GHL and Stripe sources are temporarily excluded
-- from the hard-fail list during the consolidation window.
--
--  - GHL: `bq-ingest` Cloud Run service is partially broken (U1 preflight
--    §13); Phase-2 `raw_ghl.*` last wrote 2026-04-19 14:33 and has not
--    recovered. Fix belongs to GTM's repo and is a U4b precondition, not
--    a U3 blocker. Accepted-as-is for U2–U4a per preflight sign-off.
--  - Stripe: `Raw.stripe_objects_raw` has been ~50 days stale since at
--    least 2026-03-04 (U1 preflight §10 — `stripe-backfill` Cloud Run
--    Job failing daily). Tracked in U7/U8; not cutover-blocking.
--
-- Calendly and Typeform blob sources ARE included — GTM's extractors
-- write to them hourly and both were fresh at U1 preflight. Re-add GHL
-- to this list when `bq-ingest` is repaired and the raw catches up.

with
sources as (
    select 'raw_calendly'                          as source_name,
           max(ingested_at)                        as last_ingest
    from {{ source('raw_calendly', 'calendly_objects_raw') }}
    where entity_type = 'scheduled_events'
    union all
    select 'raw_typeform',
           max(ingested_at)
    from {{ source('raw_typeform', 'typeform_objects_raw') }}
    where entity_type = 'responses'
)

select *
from sources
where timestamp_diff(current_timestamp(), last_ingest, hour) > 25

test.dee_data_ops.bridge_match_rate_floo.name: bridge_match_rate_floor
test.dee_data_ops.bridge_match_rate_floo.raw_code:
{{ config(severity = 'warn') }}

-- Asserts the per-source matched-share clears the floor documented in
-- `_bridges__docs.md` ("Target match rate ≥ 70% across the unioned
-- bridge"). Returns one row per `source_platform` whose `matched`
-- share is below the threshold; test passes when zero rows return.
--
-- Severity is `warn` because new payment processors can land with
-- low initial match rates while contact backfill catches up — the
-- ≥70% target is a tuning trigger, not a hard ship gate. Hard
-- failures still come from `bridge_payment_count_parity` (no row
-- silently dropped) and `dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns`
-- (composite PK held).

{% set match_rate_floor = 0.70 %}

with per_source as (

    select
        source_platform,
        countif(bridge_status = 'matched')                        as matched_n,
        count(*)                                                  as total_n,
        safe_divide(
            countif(bridge_status = 'matched'),
            count(*)
        )                                                         as matched_share
    from {{ ref('bridge_identity_contact_payment') }}
    group by 1

)

select
    source_platform,
    matched_n,
    total_n,
    matched_share
from per_source
where matched_share < {{ match_rate_floor }}

--

-- low initial match rates while contact backfill catches up — the
-- ≥70% target is a tuning trigger, not a hard ship gate. Hard

-- silently dropped) and `dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns`
-- (composite PK held).



with per_source as (

        source_platform,
        countif(bridge_status = 'matched')                        as matched_n,
        count(*)                                                  as total_n,
        safe_divide(
            countif(bridge_status = 'matched'),
            count(*)
        )                                                         as matched_share
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`bridge_identity_contact_payment`
    group by 1

)

select
    source_platform,

from per_source
where matched_share < 0.7

test.dee_data_ops.staging_ghl_column_ren.name: staging_ghl_column_rename_parity
test.dee_data_ops.staging_ghl_column_ren.raw_code:
-- U3 column-rename parity test: for each GHL staging model that is actually
-- producing data, verify the staging PK set equals the distinct
-- `entity_id` set in the corresponding Phase-2 raw table. Proves the
-- `entity_id AS id` rename in the source CTE is lossless end-to-end
-- (through the dedupe + parsed CTEs, out to the final PK alias).
--
-- Intentional scope:
--  - Only models with >0 rows are checked (contacts, conversations,
--    opportunities, pipelines). messages + users are 0-row upstream.
--  - This is a U3-scope structural test. Cross-project parity against
--    `dee-data-ops-prod` lives in U4a (`cutover-speed-to-lead-plumbing-
--    parity.sql`) against a frozen snapshot.
--
-- Returns zero rows when rename is lossless; any row = divergence.

with divergence as (
    select 'stg_ghl__contacts' as model,
           (select count(distinct contact_id)     from {{ ref('stg_ghl__contacts') }})     as stg_n,
           (select count(distinct entity_id)      from {{ source('ghl', 'contacts') }})    as raw_n
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__conversations',
           (select count(distinct conversation_id) from {{ ref('stg_ghl__conversations') }}),
           (select count(distinct entity_id)       from {{ source('ghl', 'conversations') }})
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__opportunities',
           (select count(distinct opportunity_id)  from {{ ref('stg_ghl__opportunities') }}),
           (select count(distinct entity_id)       from {{ source('ghl', 'opportunities') }})
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__pipelines',
           (select count(distinct pipeline_id)     from {{ ref('stg_ghl__pipelines') }}),
           (select count(distinct entity_id)       from {{ source('ghl', 'pipelines') }})
)

select *
from divergence
where stg_n <> raw_n

test.dee_data_ops.volume_drift.name: volume_drift
test.dee_data_ops.volume_drift.raw_code:
{{ config(severity='warn') }}

-- Warn-severity singular test: any mart's daily row count >10% off prior-day
-- baseline returns a row. Severity=warn means it does NOT fail the build —
-- the nightly workflow runs this test separately with --warn-error to detect
-- the warn and post a Slack soft-alert. Skips the first day (prior_count null).

with
daily as (
    select
        mart_name,
        snapshot_date,
        row_count,
        lag(row_count) over (partition by mart_name order by snapshot_date) as prior_count
    from {{ ref('mart_volume_history') }}
),

drift as (
    select
        mart_name,
        snapshot_date,
        row_count,
        prior_count,
        safe_divide(abs(row_count - prior_count), prior_count) as pct_drift
    from daily
    where snapshot_date = current_date()
      and prior_count is not null
)

select *
from drift
where pct_drift > 0.10

test.dee_data_ops.staging_shim_row_count.name: staging_shim_row_count_sanity
test.dee_data_ops.staging_shim_row_count.compiled_code:
-- U3 sanity test: each blob-shim staging model decodes at least a minimum
-- row count from its upstream blob filter. Prevents a silent-zero regression
-- where (for example) a wrong `object_type` / `entity_type` literal leaves
-- every stg model empty.
--
-- Thresholds are ground-truth row counts captured at U1 preflight
-- (2026-04-23). They are floors, not ceilings — new data landing is fine.
--
-- Excluded:
--  - stg_ghl__messages / stg_ghl__users (0 rows in raw, known upstream gap)
--  - stg_typeform__responses zero-check relies on the blob too, included
--
-- Returns zero rows when all shims are producing data; any row = regression.

with counts as (
    select 'stg_stripe__charges'    as model, count(*) as n, 3000  as floor from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_stripe__charges`
    union all
    select 'stg_stripe__customers',              count(*), 500          from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_stripe__customers`
    union all
    select 'stg_typeform__responses',            count(*), 4000         from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_typeform__responses`
    union all
    select 'stg_fathom__calls',                  count(*), 1000         from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_fathom__calls`
    union all
    select 'stg_calendly__events',               count(*), 3000         from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_calendly__events`
    union all
    select 'stg_calendly__event_invitees',       count(*), 3000         from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_calendly__event_invitees`
    union all
    select 'stg_calendly__event_types',          count(*), 1            from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_calendly__event_types`
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__contacts',                  count(*), 10000        from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__contacts`
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__opportunities',             count(*), 20000        from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__opportunities`
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__pipelines',                 count(*), 20           from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__pipelines`
    union all
    select 'stg_ghl__conversations',             count(*), 1            from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`STG`.`stg_ghl__conversations`
)

select *
from counts
where n < floor

seed.dee_data_ops.stl_lead_source_lookup.name: stl_lead_source_lookup
seed.dee_data_ops.stl_lead_source_lookup.description: Lead-source enrichment seed for the Speed-to-Lead pipeline. Maps the raw `lead_source` campaign/channel labels from `dim_contacts` to human-readable descriptions and a paid/organic flag. Only high-volume, clearly-inferrable channel-level values are classified here; campaign- specific labels require David's classification (flagged in WORKLOG "Open threads"). The `__unknown__` sentinel row handles NULL lead_source.

lead_source.name: lead_source
lead_source.description: Raw lead_source value as it appears in dim_contacts (case-sensitive match attempted; dim_source uses lower() for joins).

source_description.name: source_description
source_description.description: Human-readable description of the lead source for BI tooltips and Metabase card labels.

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.description: TRUE if the source is paid traffic; FALSE if organic/referral. NULL for the __unknown__ sentinel and for any unseeded campaign labels whose paid/organic status is ambiguous — flagged to David.

seed.dee_data_ops.ghl_sdr_roster.name: ghl_sdr_roster
seed.dee_data_ops.ghl_sdr_roster.description:
Human-maintained roster of GHL users with role assignments. GHL's built-in `role` and `type` fields are account/agency + user/admin (agency hierarchy), which is orthogonal to the functional role distinction the Speed-to-Lead metric needs. This seed is the source of truth for who counts as an SDR when attributing first outbound touch on a booking.
Valid `role` values follow the 5-role taxonomy revealed by the Master Lead Sheet oracle (columns AZ–BD): SDR, Setter, Triager, DM_Setter, Closer. Plus Owner for the client/operator accounts and unknown for users awaiting manual confirmation.
The Speed-to-Lead metric numerator filters `WHERE first_toucher.role = 'SDR'` at the mart layer (per .claude/rules/mart-naming.md). Other roles still appear in the warehouse (`fct_outreach` captures all roles) and in Page 2 attribution tiles, but only SDR touches count for the headline 5-min SLA metric.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description: GHL user id — joins to raw_ghl.users.id / conversations.assignedTo / messages.userId.

role.name: role
role.description: Functional role driving Speed-to-Lead attribution + v1.5 leaderboards.

seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_dashboard_metri.name: oracle_dashboard_metrics_20260319
seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_dashboard_metri.description: Oracle validation fixture — dashboard-level KPIs. source: Master Lead Sheet.xlsx snapshot 2026-03-19 (Dashboard tab, zero-PII aggregates).

section.name: section
section.description: Section grouping from the source tab (GHL PIPELINE, AI QUALIFICATION, SDR EOD, CLOSER EOD, SLACK, TEAM ATTRIBUTION, or blank for top-level KPIs).

value.name: value
value.description: KPI value as string (mixed types: integer, percentage, currency, timestamp).

seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_revenue_by_stag.name: oracle_revenue_by_stage_20260319
seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_revenue_by_stag.description: Oracle validation fixture — revenue and customer counts by pipeline stage. source: Master Lead Sheet.xlsx snapshot 2026-03-19.

seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_show_rate_by_ca.name: oracle_show_rate_by_campaign_20260319
seed.dee_data_ops.oracle_show_rate_by_ca.description: Oracle validation fixture — show rate and revenue by campaign source. source: Master Lead Sheet.xlsx snapshot 2026-03-19.

test.dee_data_ops.relationships_fct_call.name: relationships_fct_calls_booked_contact_sk__contact_sk__ref_dim_contacts_
test.dee_data_ops.relationships_fct_call.compiled_code:
with child as (
    select contact_sk as from_field
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`fct_calls_booked`
    where contact_sk is not null
),

parent as (
    select contact_sk as to_field
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`dim_contacts`
)

select
    from_field

from child
left join parent

test.dee_data_ops.dbt_utils_accepted_ran.name: dbt_utils_accepted_range_fct_refunds_refund_amount__True__0
test.dee_data_ops.dbt_utils_accepted_ran.compiled_code:
with meet_condition as(
  select *
  from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Core`.`fct_refunds`
),

validation_errors as (
  select *
  from meet_condition
  where
    -- never true, defaults to an empty result set. Exists to ensure any combo of the `or` clauses below succeeds
    1 = 2
    -- records with a value >= min_value are permitted. The `not` flips this to find records that don't meet the rule.
    or not refund_amount >= 0
)

source.dee_data_ops.raw_fathom.fathom_ca.name: fathom_calls_raw
source.dee_data_ops.raw_fathom.fathom_ca.source_description:
Raw landing zone for Fathom (sales call recordings + transcripts). Post-U2 (2026-04-23) staging reads from the consolidated project's legacy single-table `Raw.fathom_calls_raw`. One entity_type (`calls`), so no object-type filtering needed beyond the default — but the `entity_type` column is preserved for defensive filtering in case the GTM extractor ever multiplexes.
TODO: retire this shim when `raw_fathom.fathom__calls_raw` per-object tables are populated (U6 Phase-2 Fathom upgrade — ported from GTM with transcript fix).
Known gap (U1 preflight §9): 0% transcript coverage across 1,157 calls. `$.transcript` is null in every row. Stg passes the null through; root cause + fix are scoped to U6 (port + fix Fathom extractor).
source.dee_data_ops.raw_fathom.fathom_ca.description: GTM-extractor landing table. One row per Fathom recording. Carries the full Fathom API payload as `payload_json` plus several extracted top-level columns populated by the extractor + downstream BQML classifier (`workspace_id`, `team_id`, `title`, `ended_at_ts`, `external_participant_count`, `is_internal_only`, `is_revenue_relevant`, `classification_label`, `classification_confidence`, `classification_reason`).

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column — full Fathom recording payload. Includes `$.recording_start_time`, `$.scheduled_start_time`, `$.meeting_title`, `$.recorded_by.{email,name}`, `$.calendar_invitees[]`, `$.share_url`, `$.url`, `$.transcript` (currently null; U6 fix).

source.dee_data_ops.raw_stripe.stripe_ob.name: stripe_objects_raw
source.dee_data_ops.raw_stripe.stripe_ob.source_description:
Raw landing zone for Stripe. Post-U2 (2026-04-23) staging reads from the consolidated project's legacy single-blob table `Raw.stripe_objects_raw` via a U3 shim — each downstream staging model filters by `object_type` and JSON-decodes `payload_json`. Fivetran `raw_stripe.{charge,customer,...}` is no longer the source of truth on this project.
TODO: retire this shim when `raw_stripe.stripe__<object>_raw` per-object tables are populated (U7 Phase-2 Stripe upgrade). The U3 shim and the U7 per-object tables share the same downstream column contract, so the swap is a source-yaml + source-CTE change only.
Freshness note: Stripe raw is known ~50 days stale as of U1 preflight (stripe-backfill Cloud Run Job failing daily; preflight §10). Tracked separately; not a U3 shim concern.
source.dee_data_ops.raw_stripe.stripe_ob.description: GTM-extractor single-blob landing table. One row per Stripe object regardless of type; `object_type` column filters to charges / customers / invoices / etc. `payload_json` holds the full Stripe object as returned by the Stripe API. Seen object_types in U1 preflight §10: charges, balance_transactions, invoices, customers, subscriptions, products, prices, refunds, disputes.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column — full Stripe object payload.

source.dee_data_ops.raw_typeform.typefor.name: typeform_objects_raw
source.dee_data_ops.raw_typeform.typefor.source_description:
Raw landing zone for Typeform. Post-U2 (2026-04-23) staging reads from the consolidated project's legacy single-blob table `Raw.typeform_objects_raw` via a U3 shim — staging filters by `entity_type = 'responses'` (or `'forms'`) and JSON-decodes `payload_json`. Fivetran `raw_typeform.{response,response_answer,form}` is no longer the source of truth on this project.
TODO: retire this shim when `raw_typeform.typeform__<entity>_raw` per-object tables are populated (U9 Phase-2 Typeform upgrade).
Gap surfaced in U3: the GTM extractor does not currently persist a `form_id` top-level column or embed it in `payload_json` for `entity_type='responses'`. The response → form linkage is lost in the blob. Staging emits `form_id AS NULL` with a TODO; the real fix is the U9 extractor rewrite (either extract from the API request path or add a `parent_id`/`form_id` column to the landing table).
source.dee_data_ops.raw_typeform.typefor.description: GTM-extractor single-blob landing table. One row per Typeform object regardless of kind; `entity_type` column filters to responses / forms. `payload_json` holds the full Typeform object as returned by the Typeform API (Responses API for responses, Forms API for forms).

entity_id.name: entity_id
entity_id.description: Typeform object id. For responses, equal to the response token.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column — full Typeform API payload.

source.dee_data_ops.raw_fanbasis.fanbasi.name: fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw
source.dee_data_ops.raw_fanbasis.fanbasi.source_description:
Raw landing zone for Fanbasis. Post-U2 (2026-04-23) discovery found fresh revenue rows in the consolidated project's `Raw` dataset, while this repo still has no Fanbasis staging model.
Important distinction: `1-raw-landing/fanbasis/extract.py` remains a stub in this repo, so the observed `Raw.fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw` table is likely written by the GTM-side pipeline or another upstream writer. Before building marts, audit the payload shape and confirm the authoritative extractor path.
source.dee_data_ops.raw_fanbasis.fanbasi.description: Observed Fanbasis transaction landing table in the consolidated project. Discovery snapshot: 455 rows, fresh daily around 07:27 UTC. No staging model reads this table yet; `stg_fanbasis__transactions` is the expected first dbt-side unblocker.

entity_id.name: entity_id
entity_id.description: Fanbasis transaction id when present in the upstream landing table.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column containing the Fanbasis transaction payload.

source.dee_data_ops.ghl.users.name: users
source.dee_data_ops.ghl.users.source_description:
Raw landing zone for GoHighLevel v2 API data. Reads from per-entity VIEWS in `raw_ghl_v2`, each filtering the live single-table `Raw.ghl_objects_raw` by `entity_type` and aliasing `ingested_at AS _ingested_at` to match the legacy column shape. `Raw.ghl_objects_raw` is populated by `bq-ingest` Cloud Run service (consolidated 2026-04-28, see `services/bq-ingest/`).
Schema-level history (2026-04-29 cutover): originally `schema: raw_ghl` pointed at per-entity tables `raw_ghl.ghl__<obj>_raw`. Those tables stopped receiving writes 2026-04-19 when the bq-ingest service was retargeted to the union table `Raw.ghl_objects_raw`. The cutover here repoints staging at the new views without dropping the legacy tables (preserved as historical reference; clean up at Phase 6).
Column shape (unchanged for downstream): the views expose `(entity_id, _ingested_at, payload_json, location_id, partition_date, event_ts, updated_at_ts, source, backfill_run_id, is_backfill)`. Staging models continue to read `entity_id`, `_ingested_at`, `payload_json` and rename to `(id, _ingested_at, payload)` in their source CTEs.
Coverage notes (2026-04-29):
  - contacts:            16,031 rows in union (covered)
  - opportunities:       26,156 rows (covered)
  - conversations:       1,572 rows  (covered; legacy had 101 rows)
  - pipelines:           36 rows     (covered)
  - messages:            0 rows in union — bq-ingest does NOT poll
                         messages currently. Legacy had 17,971 rows
                         but they're not in the union; freshness is
                         disabled below until ingestion is re-enabled.
  - users:               0 rows on both paths; freshness disabled.
source.dee_data_ops.ghl.users.description: One row per GHL user (agency + account scope). ~16 rows expected; 0 rows present in `raw_ghl.ghl__users_raw` as of U1 preflight — scheduler path is not populating this entity_type on either the per-object or legacy blob table. Staging passes zero rows through until upstream fix.

entity_id.name: entity_id
entity_id.description: GHL user id (primary key of the source record).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column — full user object as returned by GHL /users/search. Cast to string in staging for downstream compatibility.

source.dee_data_ops.raw_calendly.calendl.name: calendly_objects_raw
source.dee_data_ops.raw_calendly.calendl.source_description:
Raw landing zone for Calendly. Post-U2 (2026-04-23) staging reads from the consolidated project's legacy single-blob table `Raw.calendly_objects_raw` via a U3 shim — staging filters by `entity_type IN ('scheduled_events', 'event_invitees', 'event_types')` and JSON-decodes `payload_json`.
The pre-U3 staging unioned a Fivetran source (`raw_calendly.event` / `raw_calendly.event_invitee`) with a Cloud Run poller source (`raw_calendly.scheduled_events` / `raw_calendly.invitees`). Both paths lived in the Merge-owned `dee-data-ops` project and are no longer the source of truth on this consolidated project. GTM's Cloud Run poller already landed the same data into `Raw.calendly_objects_raw` (10,882 fresh rows per U1 preflight §7); Merge's staging now routes through that shape.
The Phase-2 per-object tables `raw_calendly.calendly__*_raw` exist on this project but are empty (U1 preflight §7) — scaffolded but no writer. U3 treats this as "Phase-2 not landed for Calendly" and uses the blob-shim pattern exactly like Stripe / Typeform / Fathom.
TODO: retire this shim when the Calendly Phase-2 per-object tables start landing data (future work — not in the U6/U7/U9 Phase-2 slate). Freshness: 1h warn / 4h error matches the GTM Cloud Run poller cadence.
source.dee_data_ops.raw_calendly.calendl.description: GTM-extractor single-blob landing table. One row per Calendly object regardless of kind; `entity_type` filters to scheduled_events / event_invitees / event_types. `payload_json` holds the full Calendly API payload. `parent_id` links invitees to their parent scheduled_event.

entity_id.name: entity_id
entity_id.description: Calendly object UUID (tail of the URI).

parent_id.name: parent_id
parent_id.description: Parent UUID — for event_invitees, the parent scheduled_event. Null for scheduled_events + event_types.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row was written into BigQuery.

payload_json.name: payload_json
payload_json.description: JSON column — full Calendly API payload.

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        tables.project_id as table_catalog,
        tables.dataset_id as table_schema,
        coalesce(REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^(.+)[0-9]{8}$'), tables.table_id) as table_name,
        tables.table_id as shard_name,
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^.+([0-9]{8})$') as shard_index,
        REGEXP_CONTAINS(tables.table_id, '^.+[0-9]{8}$') and tables.type = 1 as is_date_shard,
        case
            when materialized_views.table_name is not null then 'materialized view'
            when tables.type = 1 then 'table'
            when tables.type = 2 then 'view'
            else 'external'
        end as table_type,
        tables.type = 1 as is_table,
        JSON_VALUE(table_description.option_value) as table_comment,
        tables.size_bytes,
        tables.row_count
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='__TABLES__') }} tables
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='MATERIALIZED_VIEWS') }} materialized_views
        on materialized_views.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and materialized_views.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and materialized_views.table_name = tables.table_id
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='TABLE_OPTIONS') }} table_description
        on table_description.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and table_description.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and table_description.table_name = tables.table_id
        and table_description.option_name = 'description'
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.name: _bigquery__get_tables_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_tables_sql() %}
    select distinct
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        is_date_shard,
        table_type,
        is_table,
        table_comment
    from table_shards
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        max(shard_name) as latest_shard_name,
        min(shard_index) as shard_min,
        max(shard_index) as shard_max,
        count(shard_index) as shard_count,
        sum(size_bytes) as size_bytes,
        sum(row_count) as row_count
    from table_shards
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_columns_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_columns_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        columns.table_catalog,
        columns.table_schema,
        columns.table_name as shard_name,
        coalesce(paths.field_path, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        -- invent a row number to account for nested fields
        -- BQ does not treat these nested properties as independent fields
        row_number() over (
            partition by
                columns.table_catalog,
                columns.table_schema,
                columns.table_name
            order by
                columns.ordinal_position,
                paths.field_path
        ) as column_index,
        coalesce(paths.data_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        paths.description as column_comment,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then 1 else 0 end as is_partitioning_column,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then paths.field_path end as partition_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then 1 else 0 end as is_clustering_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then paths.field_path end as cluster_column,
        columns.clustering_ordinal_position
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMNS') }} columns
    join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS') }} paths
        on paths.table_catalog = columns.table_catalog
        and paths.table_schema = columns.table_schema
        and paths.table_name = columns.table_name
        and paths.column_name = columns.column_name
    where columns.ordinal_position is not null
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        shard_name,
        max(is_partitioning_column) = 1 as is_partitioned,
        max(partition_column) as partition_column,
        max(is_clustering_column) = 1 as is_clustered,
        array_to_string(
            array_agg(
                cluster_column ignore nulls
                order by clustering_ordinal_position
            ), ', '
        ) as clustering_columns
    from columns
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.name: _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql() %}
    select
        tables.table_catalog as table_database,
        tables.table_schema,
        case
            when tables.is_date_shard then concat(tables.table_name, '*')
            else tables.table_name
        end as table_name,
        tables.table_type,
        tables.table_comment,
        -- coalesce column metadata fields to ensure they are non-null for catalog generation
        -- external table columns are not present in COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS
        coalesce(columns.column_name, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        coalesce(columns.column_index, 1) as column_index,
        coalesce(columns.column_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        coalesce(columns.column_comment, '') as column_comment,

        'Shard count' as `stats__date_shards__label`,
        table_stats.shard_count as `stats__date_shards__value`,
        'The number of date shards in this table' as `stats__date_shards__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shards__include`,

        'Shard (min)' as `stats__date_shard_min__label`,
        table_stats.shard_min as `stats__date_shard_min__value`,
        'The first date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_min__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_min__include`,

        'Shard (max)' as `stats__date_shard_max__label`,
        table_stats.shard_max as `stats__date_shard_max__value`,
        'The last date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_max__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_max__include`,

        '# Rows' as `stats__num_rows__label`,
        table_stats.row_count as `stats__num_rows__value`,
        'Approximate count of rows in this table' as `stats__num_rows__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_rows__include`,

        'Approximate Size' as `stats__num_bytes__label`,
        table_stats.size_bytes as `stats__num_bytes__value`,
        'Approximate size of table as reported by BigQuery' as `stats__num_bytes__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_bytes__include`,

        'Partitioned By' as `stats__partitioning_type__label`,
        column_stats.partition_column as `stats__partitioning_type__value`,
        'The partitioning column for this table' as `stats__partitioning_type__description`,
        column_stats.is_partitioned as `stats__partitioning_type__include`,

        'Clustered By' as `stats__clustering_fields__label`,
        column_stats.clustering_columns as `stats__clustering_fields__value`,
        'The clustering columns for this table' as `stats__clustering_fields__description`,
        column_stats.is_clustered as `stats__clustering_fields__include`

    from tables
    join table_stats
        on table_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and table_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and table_stats.table_name = tables.table_name
    left join column_stats
        on column_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and column_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and column_stats.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
    left join columns
        on columns.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and columns.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and columns.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
{% endmacro %}
